name: Executive and Key Employee Retention
description: We may be unable to attract, retain and motivate our executives and key employees.

name: Expansion Strain on Resources
description: Rapidly and significantly expanding global operations, including increasing product and service offerings and scaling infrastructure, places significant strain on management, personnel, operations, systems, technical performance, financial resources, and internal financial control and reporting functions.

name: Expansion into New Products and Services
description: Expansion into new products, services, technologies, and geographic regions subjects the company to additional risks including limited experience in newer market segments, technology challenges, service disruptions, and potential write-downs of investments.

name: Export Control Restrictions
description: Semiconductors associated with AI, including GPUs, are increasingly the focus of export control restrictions. Controls may prohibit exporting products to customers in one or more markets, including China, negatively impacting manufacturing, testing, warehousing, revenue, and financial results.

name: Export Control and Trade Sanctions Risk
description: Government actions including trade protection and national security policies, tariffs, import/export regulations, trade and economic sanctions, quotas or other trade barriers could affect ability to ship products, provide services, and fulfill contractual obligations. Violations of export control laws could result in material adverse impact on business.

name: Export License Requirements
description: New U.S. government license requirements impacting certain exports of data center products to China (including Hong Kong and Macau) and Russia make it difficult to manage operations and forecast operating results.

name: Export and Import Regulation Risk
description: The Company is subject to changing regulations relating to the export and import of its products, which could result in products being banned, delayed, or prohibited from importation. Such outcomes could materially adversely affect the Company's business, reputation, results of operations, and financial condition.

name: Extended Payment Terms and Customer Bad Debt
description: Extended payment term arrangements with certain customers, the inability of some customers to make required payments, ability to obtain credit insurance, and customer bad debt write-offs pose financial risks.

name: Extensive Government Regulation and Compliance
description: Business is subject to extensive, complex, overlapping, and frequently changing rules, regulations, and legal interpretations. Failure to comply could materially harm the business.

name: Extreme Heat Events and Peak Loads
description: The Utility's electric distribution system has experienced multiple major outage-causing events associated with extreme heat and peak loads, and peak loads are expected to increase with rising temperatures due to direct impacts on equipment and growing electricity demand driven by air conditioning usage.

name: FERC Regulatory Risk
description: The FERC has authority to impose fines of up to $1 million per day for violations of certain federal statutes and regulations, and regulates electric transmission, hydroelectric licensing, and natural gas transportation.

name: FERC Regulatory Violations
description: FERC has authority to impose fines of up to $1 million per day for violations of certain federal statutes and regulations.

name: Fabless Manufacturing Dependency
description: NVIDIA relies on key third-party suppliers for all phases of manufacturing including wafer fabrication, assembly, testing, and packaging, which introduces supply chain risk.

name: Failure to Implement New Information Systems
description: Any failure to successfully implement new information systems and technologies, or improvements or upgrades to existing information systems and technologies in a timely manner could adversely impact business, internal controls, results of operations, and financial condition.

name: Failure to Meet Evolving Industry Needs
description: Failure to meet the evolving needs of our industry and markets may adversely impact our financial results. Our accelerated computing platforms experience rapid changes in technology, customer requirements, competitive products, and industry standards.

name: Failure to Mitigate Unsafe Conditions Risk
description: Failure to take expeditious or sufficient action to mitigate operating conditions, facilities, or equipment identified as unsafe, which then leads to a catastrophic event.

name: Federal GHG Regulation Risk
description: The EPA is using its authority under the Clean Air Act to address GHG emissions, and the Biden Administration has signaled intent to use executive and regulatory authorities to reduce emissions, including potential new methane emission rules for oil and gas sector and stricter GHG standards for power plants.

name: Financial Condition Risk
description: Financial condition risks include substantial indebtedness and related restrictions, appeals of the Confirmation Order, potential dilution to holders of PG&E Corporation common stock, substantial stock sales by existing stockholders, ownership and transfer restrictions, and tax-related risks including the grantor trust election for the Fire Victim Trust and the inability to use certain tax attributes.

name: Financial and Fraud Risk
description: The payments platform faces risk of financial losses and fraud affecting merchants and consumers.

name: Financing Cost Risk
description: If costs recorded in regulatory accounts continue to increase or the delay between incurring and recovering costs lengthens, PG&E Corporation and the Utility may incur additional financing costs.

name: Financing Risk
description: The Company requires a higher volume of royalty revenues to raise sufficient operating cash flows and may need to raise additional funds via equity or debt financing.

name: Financing and Capital Markets Risk
description: Future cash flows from financing activities will be affected by the timing and outcome of AB 1054 securitization transactions, the potential sale of a minority interest in Pacific Generation, dividend payments, and equity contributions.

name: Fire Victim Trust Grantor Trust Election
description: Because PG&E Corporation and the Utility have elected to treat the Fire Victim Trust as a grantor trust, the application of the Ownership Restrictions will be determined based on a number of shares outstanding that could differ materially from the number of shares reported as outstanding in its periodic reports under the Exchange Act.

name: Fire Victim Trust Share Issuance Risk
description: On the Emergence Date, PG&E Corporation issued to the Fire Victim Trust shares equal to 22.19% of outstanding common stock. Additional shares may be required to be issued to the Fire Victim Trust, resulting in further dilution to existing shareholders and potential decline in trading price.

name: Fixed Cost Growth
description: Fixed costs include expenses necessary to build and run technology infrastructure, enhance online stores, web services, electronic devices, and digital offerings, and build and optimize the fulfillment network. Unnecessary growth in these fixed costs represents a material risk to the company.

name: Fluctuations in Operating Results and Growth Rate
description: The company is not always able to accurately forecast its growth rate, bases expense levels and investment plans on sales estimates, and faces risks from softening demand, changes in customer preferences, weakening economies, competitive pressures, and other factors that may cause revenue and operating results to fluctuate.

name: Foreign Cash Repatriation Tax Risk
description: Approximately $1.38 billion of cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities held outside the U.S. for which no related foreign or state taxes have been accrued; repatriation could trigger additional tax obligations.

name: Foreign Corrupt Practices Risk
description: The company faces compliance obligations under the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and other applicable U.S. and foreign laws that prohibit corrupt payments to government officials and other third parties. Failure to comply with these laws could expose the company to significant legal and financial risk.

name: Foreign Currency Exchange Fee Decline
description: Declines in foreign currency exchange fees contributed to slower transaction revenue growth relative to TPV and number of payment transactions in 2022.

name: Foreign Currency Exchange Rate Risk
description: Revenues from Rest of the world were unfavorably impacted by changes in foreign currency exchange rates, primarily because the U.S. dollar strengthened relative to the Euro, Brazilian real, Australian dollar, and Japanese yen.

name: Foreign Currency Risk - Investments
description: A 10% decrease in foreign exchange rates could result in a loss of $29 million in fair value of foreign currency-denominated investments.

name: Foreign Currency Weakness
description: The weakness in foreign currencies relative to the U.S. dollar accounted for more than the entire year-over-year decrease in total net sales during FY2023.

name: Foreign Currency and Interest Rate Fluctuation Risk
description: Macroeconomic conditions may expose PayPal to fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates or interest rates that could materially and adversely affect its financial results.

name: Foreign Earnings Tax Risk
description: The company's income tax rate is significantly affected by the tax rates that apply to foreign earnings, including provisions related to the global intangible low-taxed income (GILTI) tax and the base erosion anti-abuse tax.

name: Foreign Exchange Rate Fluctuations
description: Fluctuations in foreign exchange rates may adversely affect the company's net sales, operating expenses, and operating income.

name: Foreign Exchange Rate Volatility
description: Changes in foreign exchange rates significantly affect reported results and consolidated trends, as fluctuations in the U.S. Dollar relative to currencies in international locations impact consolidated net sales and operating expenses. Continued currency volatility may significantly affect reported results and period-over-period comparisons.

name: Foreign Government Retaliation Risk
description: Export restrictions may provoke responses from foreign governments, including China, that negatively impact the supply chain or ability to provide products and services to customers worldwide, substantially reducing revenue.

name: Foreign Tax Exposure
description: Cash held by foreign subsidiaries may be subject to U.S. taxation under Subpart F, GILTI, or the one-time transition tax, and subsequent repatriations may incur state income or foreign withholding taxes.

name: Forward-Looking Statements Risk
description: PG&E Corporation and the Utility are subject to various risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from forward-looking statements based on management's current estimates, expectations, and projections.

name: Foundry and Manufacturing Risk
description: NVIDIA depends on a limited number of foundries to manufacture semiconductor wafers, and risks include lack of guaranteed supply, failure to procure raw materials, failure to develop high-quality process technologies, and potential production delays from losing a supplier.

name: Franchise Agreement Risk
description: The Utility has franchise agreements with approximately 300 cities and counties permitting installation and operation of electric and natural gas facilities. Most agreements are for indeterminate terms with no expiration date, and the Utility pays annual fees for use of public streets and highways.

name: Fraud Risk
description: Fraud schemes related to Venmo products and services may result in increased losses, negatively impacting transaction loss metrics.

name: Fraud and Abuse Risk
description: Users may engage in fraudulent or abusive activities through cloud-based services, including unauthorized use of accounts, stolen credit cards, failure to pay for services, cryptocurrency mining, or launching cyberattacks. Ineffective detection may result in adverse revenue impacts or reputational damage.

name: Fraud and Abusive Behavior Risk
description: Third parties may attempt to abuse access to and misuse PayPal's payments services to commit fraud, including creating fictitious accounts using stolen or synthetic identities, making transactions with stolen financial instruments, and fraudulently inducing users into fraudulent transactions. Measures to detect and reduce fraud risk may not be effective against new and evolving forms of fraud.

name: Fraudulent Activities
description: Fraudulent activities including account takeover, identity theft, stolen financial information, and malicious actions by counterparties represent a significant risk to merchants, consumers, and their payment partners.

name: Fraudulent or Unlawful Seller Activities
description: Sellers may fraudulently collect payments, sell unlawful, counterfeit, pirated, or stolen goods, or otherwise violate policies. When prevention processes fail, it can harm the business, damage reputation, and result in civil or criminal liability.

name: Fulfillment Network Expansion Costs
description: Fulfillment costs are impacted by increased investments in the fulfillment network, variable costs corresponding with higher product and service sales volumes and inventory levels, and increased wage rates and incentives.

name: Fulfillment Network Optimization Risk
description: Failures to adequately predict customer demand or otherwise optimize and operate fulfillment network and data centers successfully result in excess or insufficient capacity, service interruptions, increased costs, and impairment charges.

name: Funding Mix Risk
description: Changes in product mix, merchant mix, regional mix, and funding mix can affect the transaction expense rate. Macroeconomic shifts may also alter consumer spending behavior and the types of funding sources used.

name: Future Wildfire Claims Funding Risk
description: There are risks related to the timing and amounts of available funds to pay eligible claims for liabilities arising from future wildfires.

name: Future Wildfire Liability
description: The Utility could be alleged or determined to be a cause of future wildfires in its service area, potentially resulting in significant liability including under the doctrine of inverse condemnation.

name: Future cash flow uncertainty
description: Projections of future cash needs and cash flows are subject to substantial uncertainty.

name: GDPR Violation Risk
description: Irish Data Protection Commission investigating LinkedIn's targeted advertising practices for potential GDPR violations, with a preliminary draft decision proposing a fine.

name: GDPR and CCPA Compliance Risk
description: Privacy laws such as GDPR and CCPA require customers to impose specific contractual restrictions on service providers. A breach exposing or altering personal data could harm customer relationships and subject the company to litigation, regulatory action, or fines.

name: GDPR and UK GDPR Compliance Risk
description: The EU GDPR and UK GDPR govern strict handling of personal data within the EEA and United Kingdom. Non-compliance could result in penalties of up to EUR20 million or 4% of worldwide revenue, whichever is greater, and litigation from individuals or consumer protection organizations.

name: GFN Security Breach
description: Breaches of GFN security measures, which have occurred in the past, could expose partners to loss or misuse of proprietary game source code, damage both NVIDIA and its partners, and expose NVIDIA to potential litigation and liability.

name: GHG Cap-and-Trade Compliance Risk
description: The Utility faces compliance obligations under the cap-and-trade program as a natural gas supplier, requiring it to obtain emission allowances or offset credits to cover GHG emissions attributable to combustion of natural gas delivered to customers.

name: GHG Emission Regulation Risk
description: CARB is responsible for setting and monitoring GHG and other emission limits, and adopting regulations to gradually reduce GHG emissions in California.

name: GHG Emissions Compliance Costs
description: If the Utility increases its reliance on conventional generation resources to replace hydroelectric generation and meet increased customer demand, it may become more costly to comply with greenhouse gas emissions limits.

name: GHG Emissions and Climate Change
description: Significant GHG emissions from fossil fuel-fired plants, natural gas compressor stations, distribution fugitive natural gas, and customer natural gas use pose regulatory and environmental risks under mandatory reporting to CARB and EPA.

name: GO 95 and GO 165 Compliance Failures
description: The Utility continues to evaluate whether there are additional failures to comply with GO 95 and GO 165 beyond those identified in submitted self-reports, related to wood pole inspections and replacements.

name: Gaming Competition
description: Xbox and cloud gaming services face competition from various online gaming ecosystems and game streaming services, including those operated by Amazon, Apple, Meta, and Tencent.

name: Gas System Capacity Failure Risk
description: Failure to maintain adequate capacity to meet customer demand on the gas system can result in customer curtailments, gas outages, gas surges, serious personal injury, or loss of life.

name: Gas System Safety Risk
description: Risk of gas system incidents including pipeline explosions, workforce serious injuries and fatalities (SIF-A), and public safety incidents related to gas operations.

name: General Litigation and Government Inquiries Risk
description: The company is regularly subject to claims, lawsuits, arbitration proceedings, and government and regulatory investigations alleging violations of laws and regulations across a broad range of areas, including competition, antitrust, intellectual property, privacy, data protection, information security, anti-money laundering, sanctions, consumer protection, securities, tax, and employment matters.

name: Generative AI Security Risks
description: The increasing use of generative AI models in internal systems may create new attack vectors for adversaries, and features relying on generative AI may be susceptible to unanticipated security threats from sophisticated actors.

name: Geographic Concentration Risk
description: With corporate headquarters, large data center capacity, and research and development activities concentrated in California, and critical operations and suppliers located in Asia, the company's operations are vulnerable to natural disasters such as earthquakes and wildfires.

name: Geographic Revenue Concentration
description: NVIDIA derives significant revenue from outside the United States, representing 69% of total revenue in FY2023 and 84% in FY2022, with notable declines driven by China and Taiwan in the Data Center and Gaming segments.

name: Geopolitical Conditions
description: Global economic and geopolitical conditions may give rise to or amplify risks to the company's business.

name: Geopolitical Conflict Risk
description: Periods of intense diplomatic or armed conflict, such as the ongoing conflict in Ukraine, may result in new and rapidly evolving sanctions and trade restrictions, as well as negative impacts to regional trade ecosystems among customers, partners, and Microsoft.

name: Geopolitical Instability
description: Abrupt political change, terrorist activity, and armed conflict—such as the ongoing conflict in Ukraine—pose a risk of general economic disruption, increasing operating costs and negatively impacting the ability to sell to and collect from customers in affected markets. Geopolitical instability may also lead to sanctions and restrict the ability to conduct business in certain markets.

name: Geopolitical Risk
description: Geopolitical tensions involving Taiwan and China, which comprise a significant portion of revenue and where suppliers, contract manufacturers, and assembly partners critical to supply continuity are located.

name: Geopolitical Risk - Russia/Ukraine
description: In response to the war in Ukraine, economic sanctions and export control measures blocked the passage of products, services, and support into Russia, Belarus, and certain regions of Ukraine; direct sales to Russia were stopped and business operations there were closed in fiscal year 2023. The ongoing conflict has also adversely impacted end-customer sales in the EMEA region.

name: Geopolitical Tensions
description: Tensions between governments, including the U.S. and China, have in the past led to tariffs and other restrictions being imposed on the Company's business. If disputes and conflicts further escalate, actions by governments could be significantly more severe and restrictive.

name: Geopolitical Tensions and Conflicts
description: Worldwide geopolitical tensions and conflicts, including in China, Hong Kong, Israel, Korea, and Taiwan where product components are manufactured and assembled, may result in changing regulatory requirements and other disruptions impacting operations, product demand, access to global markets, hiring, and profitability.

name: Geopolitical and Macroeconomic Risks
description: The company faces material adverse effects from a broad range of domestic and international risks, including economic and political instability, government lockdowns, COVID-19, differing legal standards, capital market disruptions, counter-inflation policies, currency fluctuations, natural disasters, acts of war, terrorism, and other catastrophic events.

name: Geopolitical and Political Instability
description: Geopolitical tensions, political instability, changes in government, or adverse political developments in major countries where NVIDIA does business could harm its business, financial condition, and results of operations.

name: Global Business Operational and Economic Risk
description: The global nature of the business creates operational, economic, and geopolitical risks. Results of operations may be affected by global, regional, and local economic developments, monetary policy, inflation, recession, and political and military disputes.

name: Global Data Localization and Sovereignty Risk
description: Jurisdictions around the world, including China, India, and various U.S. states, have adopted or are considering laws imposing obligations regarding the collection, handling, and transfer of personal data, potentially requiring data sovereignty and localization measures.

name: Global Economic Conditions
description: Changes in global economic conditions and customer demand and spending could adversely affect the company's business performance.

name: Global Economic and Geopolitical Conditions
description: Changes in global economic and geopolitical conditions may adversely impact customer demand and spending.

name: Global Pandemic Risk
description: The occurrence of regional epidemics or a global pandemic, such as COVID-19, may adversely affect operations, financial condition, and results of operations.

name: Global Trade / Export Controls Risk
description: New US government license requirements impact exports to China (including Hong Kong and Macau) and Russia of A100, H100, DGX and related products. Competitive position may be harmed if customers in China do not purchase alternative offerings or develop internal solutions.

name: Global and Regional Economic Conditions Risk
description: Adverse global and regional economic conditions—including turmoil in banking or financial markets, credit tightening, extreme market volatility, higher unemployment, high consumer debt levels, recessionary or inflationary pressures, supply chain issues, and reduced consumer confidence—could harm PayPal's business.

name: Going Concern
description: The Company has not attained profitable operations and is dependent upon obtaining financing to pursue acquisitions and activities. During the year ended April 30, 2023, the Company incurred a net loss of $1,774,179, used cash of $872,226 for operating activities, and carried a working capital deficit of $1,741,434 and accumulated deficit of $16,033,070, raising substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern.

name: Goodwill and Intangibles Valuation Risk
description: Valuation of assets acquired in a business combination requires significant estimates and assumptions that are inherently uncertain and unpredictable, and may not reflect unanticipated events.

name: Goodwill or Intangible Asset Impairment
description: If goodwill or amortizable intangible assets become impaired, the company may be required to record a significant charge to earnings, negatively affecting results of operations.

name: Government Contract Risk
description: Government contracts present risks including audits, investigations, suspension or debarment, civil and criminal fines, contract rescission, termination without cause, and periodic funding reductions or cancellations that could adversely impact results of operations, financial condition, and reputation.

name: Government Contracts and Procurement Risk
description: The company's contracts with U.S., state, local, and foreign government entities are subject to procurement regulations and audits; violations could result in civil and criminal penalties, administrative sanctions, contract termination, forfeiture of profits, and debarment from future government business.

name: Government Litigation and Regulatory Activity
description: Government agencies scrutinize the company under U.S. and foreign competition laws in markets such as the EU, U.S., and China, which may result in fines or hinder the ability to provide software benefits to consumers.

name: Government Policy Risk
description: Government actions or changes in governmental policies, such as increased restrictions on gaming usage, impacting product demand.

name: Government Regulation
description: The oil and gas business is subject to extensive governmental regulation under which rates of production from wells may be fixed, and governmental regulation may limit or affect the market for wells' production and the price which may be paid for that production. Regulations relating to environmental matters could also affect operations.

name: Government Regulation Risk
description: New or changing laws and regulations, including changes to their interpretation and implementation, as well as increased penalties and enforcement actions related to non-compliance, could have a material adverse impact on the company's business, results of operations, and financial condition.

name: Gray Market Competition
description: New or previously sold products may be resold online or on the unauthorized gray market, making demand forecasting difficult and competing with new products and distribution channels.

name: Gross Margin Volatility
description: The Company's future gross margins can be impacted by a variety of factors, and gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure.

name: Groundwater Contamination
description: Groundwater at the Utility's Hinkley and Topock natural gas compressor stations contains hexavalent chromium as a result of past operating practices, requiring remediation and environmental abatement.

name: Hazardous Substance Compliance Risk
description: The Utility's facilities are subject to EPA regulations including RCRA and CERCLA, imposing strict liability for release of hazardous substances on owners, operators, and corporate successors.

name: Highly Competitive Global Markets
description: Global markets for the Company's products and services are highly competitive and subject to rapid technological change, with aggressive price competition, downward pressure on gross margins, and rapid adoption of technological advancements by competitors.

name: Hinkley Natural Gas Compressor Site Remediation
description: Costs to remediate groundwater contamination near the Hinkley natural gas compressor site and abate effects of contamination are not recoverable through rates or insurance, and may have a material effect on PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows.

name: Holding Company Dependency Risk
description: As a holding company, PG&E Corporation has no business operations of its own and is dependent on the operations and funds of its subsidiaries, including cash flows from those subsidiaries, to meet its obligations and service any debt.

name: Hydroelectric Generation Risk
description: Changing precipitation dynamics, including diminishing water availability and altered runoff timing during extreme drought, pose risks to hydroelectric generation, operations, and revenue. Extreme rain events also heighten the risk of hydropower asset damage or failure due to flooding.

name: IP Protection Risk
description: Laws in certain foreign countries, including various countries in Asia, may not protect products or intellectual property rights to the same extent as U.S. laws, increasing the risk of piracy of technology and products.

name: IP Theft and Piracy Risk
description: Risk of piracy of technology and products due to foreign laws not protecting IP rights to the same extent as United States law. Theft or unauthorized use of trade secrets and confidential information could harm competitive position and reduce acceptance of products.

name: IT Supply Chain Malware
description: Malicious actors may exploit the IT supply chain to introduce malware through software updates or compromised supplier accounts or hardware.

name: IT System Failures and Network Disruptions
description: The Company and its global supply chain are dependent on complex information technology systems and are exposed to information technology system failures or network disruptions caused by natural disasters, accidents, power disruptions, telecommunications failures, acts of terrorism or war, computer viruses, physical or electronic break-ins, ransomware or other cybersecurity incidents, or other events or disruptions.

name: Illegal Activities Risk
description: Users may attempt to use PayPal's payments platform for illegal activities or improper uses, including money laundering, terrorist financing, sanctions evasion, illegal online gambling, fraudulent sales, illegal telemarketing, illegal sales of prescription medications, and piracy of software, movies, music, and other copyrighted content.

name: Illegal Platform Activity
description: PayPal's payments platform may be used for illegal or improper activities, including illegal telemarketing, sales of prescription medications, piracy, bank fraud, child pornography, human trafficking, securities fraud, and pyramid or Ponzi schemes. Such misuse may subject PayPal to claims, lawsuits, and government investigations.

name: Immigration and Work Permit Regulations
description: Changes in immigration and work permit regulations or their administration could impair the ability to attract and retain qualified employees.

name: Impairment of Key Relationships Risk
description: Impairment of relationships with, or loss of employees, vendors and customers as a result of acquisitions or investments.

name: Inability to Keep Pace with Technological Change
description: If the company cannot keep pace with rapid technological developments to provide new and innovative products and services, revenues could decline.

name: Inability to Protect Information in Products and Services
description: Changes in law or interpretations may weaken the ability to prevent third parties from scraping or gathering information or content through bots or other measures, diminishing the value of products and services.

name: Inability to Protect Intellectual Property
description: Effective protection of proprietary rights, including trademarks, copyrights, domain names, trade dress, patents, and trade secrets, may not be available in every jurisdiction, and third parties may infringe or misappropriate the Company's intellectual property.

name: Inaccurate Key Metrics
description: Real or perceived inaccuracies in key metrics may harm PayPal's reputation and negatively affect its business. These metrics are calculated using internal company data compiled from multiple systems, presenting inherent challenges in measurement at global scale.

name: Inadequate Account Security
description: Adversaries that acquire user account information may compromise user accounts, and inadequate account security practices can result in unauthorized access. User activity may also introduce ransomware or other malicious software that impacts customer use of products or services.

name: Inadequate Emergency Preparedness
description: Inadequate emergency preparedness plans and failure to respond effectively to catastrophic events, leading to public or employee harm or extended outages.

name: Inadequate Security Measures
description: Security measures including encryption and authentication technologies may not be sufficient and may be vulnerable to hacking, ransomware attacks, employee error, malfeasance, system error, faulty password management or other irregularities.

name: Inclusive Culture Risk
description: If the Company is unable to nurture its distinctive and inclusive culture, it could materially adversely affect its ability to recruit and retain highly skilled employees, and could adversely affect business, reputation, results of operations and financial condition.

name: Income Tax Contingencies
description: Potential United States and foreign income tax contingencies based on estimate of whether additional taxes may be due, including uncertainties in application of complex tax laws.

name: Income Tax Risk
description: Differences in tax determinations will impact the provision for income taxes and the effective tax rate. The company is subject to continuous examination of income tax returns by the IRS and other tax authorities, which may assert additional assessments.

name: Increased Borrowing Costs
description: Potential longer-term impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic include higher credit spreads, increased borrowing costs, and incremental financing needs.

name: Increased Competition for Traffic Sources
description: Increased or decreased competition for traffic sources, or shifts in the mix of channels, may result in corresponding changes in marketing costs.

name: Increased Fulfillment and Shipping Costs
description: Increased fulfillment and shipping costs in 2022, driven by investments in the fulfillment network, higher transportation costs, and increased wage rates, negatively impacted North America and International operating income.

name: Increased Operating Costs
description: Rising operating costs in 2022, particularly across the North America and International segments, were driven by seasonal demand volumes, increased transportation and utility costs, and higher wage rates.

name: Increased Security Expenses Risk
description: To defend against cyber-attacks, the company must continuously engineer more secure products and enhance security and reliability features, which is expected to result in increased expenses.

name: Increased customer rates
description: Increased customer rates present a risk of negatively impacting the company's ability to retain customers and recover costs.

name: Increasing Commodity Costs
description: Increases in the Utility's commodity costs directly impact customer bills, including electricity and natural gas procurement costs recovered through rates as pass-through costs.

name: Increasing Regulatory Authority and Enforcement
description: Regulators globally are increasingly exercising regulatory authority, oversight, and enforcement in a manner that impacts the company's business.

name: Indebtedness Risk
description: Risks associated with existing and future indebtedness, including the need to use significant cash flow to service debt, reduced flexibility in business planning, limits on additional financing, and potential acceleration of repayment if covenants are breached.

name: Indemnification Risk
description: Agreements entered into by the Company sometimes include indemnification provisions which can subject the Company to costs and damages in the event of a claim against an indemnified third party.

name: India Regulatory Risk
description: India restricts ownership or control of Indian companies by foreign entities involved in online multi-brand retail trading activities, impacting Amazon's operations on www.amazon.in.

name: India Withholding Tax Risk
description: The company historically paid India withholding taxes on software sales. The India Supreme Court ruled in March 2021 that software sales are not subject to India withholding taxes, resulting in a tax benefit for fiscal years 1996 through 2016.

name: Industrial Accidents at Suppliers
description: The Company's operations are subject to the risks of industrial accidents at its suppliers and contract manufacturers, which could disrupt operations and supply chain.

name: Ineffective Records Management
description: An ineffective records management program resulting in failure to construct, operate, and maintain a utility system safely and prudently.

name: Inflation
description: Inflation presents a risk of increasing the company's costs and adversely impacting its operations and financial results.

name: Inflation Reduction Act Tax Impact
description: The Inflation Reduction Act imposes a 15% corporate alternative minimum tax on adjusted financial statement income for corporations with average adjusted financial statement income exceeding $1.0 billion, which could substantially increase PG&E Corporation and the Utility's federal income tax liability.

name: Inflation Risk
description: Long-term inflationary pressures may increase prices for equipment, materials, supplies, labor, contractor services, and variable-rate debt, adversely impacting the Utility's financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows.

name: Inflation and Interest Rates
description: Inflation and interest rate changes may materially affect the company's financial results.

name: Inflationary Pressures on Costs
description: Increases in the prices of transportation (including fuel), energy products, commodities like paper and packing supplies and hardware products, and technology infrastructure products, including as a result of inflationary pressures, may negatively impact operating results.

name: Information Dissemination Risk
description: The company is subject to claims relating to information disseminated through its online services, including allegations of defamation, libel, privacy invasion, and intellectual property infringement. If legal protections for online dissemination are invalidated or modified, or if measures to detect and block harmful activity on its payments platform prove insufficient, the company could be directly harmed.

name: Information Security Risk
description: Information security risks for global payments and technology companies have increased significantly in recent years. There can be no assurance that security measures will provide sufficient security or prevent breaches or attacks.

name: Information Technology Systems Risk
description: The Utility relies on complex IT systems to process sensitive information including financial, customer energy usage, billing, and personal information. Third-party vendors could cease to exist, fail to establish adequate processes, or experience security incidents. Disruptions could impact ability to track revenues and maintain effective internal controls over financial reporting.

name: Insufficient Insurance Coverage
description: Insurance policies maintained to offset financial impact from cybersecurity risks may be insufficient to compensate for all losses caused by security breaches and other damage to or unavailability of systems.

name: Insufficient Regulatory Revenue Authorization
description: PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows could be materially affected if the CPUC or the FERC does not authorize sufficient revenues for the Utility or if the amount of actual costs incurred differs from the forecast or authorized costs embedded in rates.

name: Insurance Coverage Insufficiency Risk
description: Recorded liabilities in connection with the 2019 Kincade fire and the 2021 Dixie fire have already exceeded potential amounts recoverable under applicable insurance policies, and liability insurance would not cover restitution payments ordered by the court.

name: Insurance Coverage Limitations
description: Some insurance policies have large deductibles and broad exclusions, and insurance providers may be unable or unwilling to pay a claim. Losses not covered by insurance may be large, which could harm results of operations and financial condition.

name: Intellectual Property Dependency
description: The company may need to seek or renew licenses relating to various aspects of products and services, though it believes such licenses can generally be obtained on commercially reasonable terms.

name: Intellectual Property Infringement Claims
description: Customers and partners require indemnification for certain intellectual property infringement claims, which could increase costs and may require payment of significant damages if there is an adverse ruling.

name: Intellectual Property Licensing Risk
description: Many products and services include intellectual property owned by third parties, and there is no guarantee that licenses could be obtained in the future on reasonable terms or at all.

name: Intellectual Property Protection Risk
description: Protecting intellectual property rights and combating unlicensed copying and use of software on a global basis is difficult. Changes in law may weaken the ability to prevent use of patented technology or collect royalties, and source code leaks could enable competitors to copy functionality.

name: Intellectual Property Rights Risk
description: Efforts to protect intellectual property rights could result in substantial costs, and the company's competitive position could be harmed if those efforts are unsuccessful or if the company is prohibited from making or selling products. Additional risks include patent infringement lawsuits, indemnification obligations, and potential suspension of product manufacturing and sales.

name: Intellectual Property Risk
description: The company faces risks related to intellectual property.

name: Intellectual Property Royalty Pressure
description: Certain foreign governments, particularly in China and other countries in Asia, have advanced arguments under their competition laws that exert downward pressure on royalties for intellectual property.

name: Intense Competition
description: Businesses are rapidly evolving, intensely competitive, and subject to changing technologies, shifting user needs, and frequent introductions of new products and services. Competitors range from large established companies to emerging start-ups in search, online advertising, wireless mobile devices, and operating systems.

name: Intense Competition in Global Payments Industry
description: The global payments industry is highly competitive, dynamic, and innovative, with competition likely to intensify as new competitors emerge, businesses form combinations and partnerships, and established companies expand into new segments.

name: Interchange Fee Regulatory Risk
description: Interchange fees associated with four-party payment systems are under review or challenge in various jurisdictions, and fees that PayPal collects may become subject to regulatory challenge, including under the EU Multilateral Interchange Fee Regulation.

name: Intercompany Foreign Exchange Risk
description: Intercompany balances denominated in various foreign currencies expose the company to foreign exchange risk. Based on intercompany balances as of December 31, 2022, an assumed 5%, 10%, and 20% adverse change in foreign exchange rates would result in losses of $275 million, $555 million, and $1.1 billion, respectively.

name: Interest Rate Risk
description: Adverse changes in market interest rates could decrease the fair value of marketable securities. A hypothetical 1.00% (100 basis points) increase in interest rates would have resulted in a decrease in the fair value of marketable securities of approximately $1.2 billion and $1.3 billion as of December 31, 2014 and December 31, 2015 respectively.

name: Interest Rate Risk on Long-Term Debt
description: PG&E Corporation's net loss increased in 2022 compared to 2021 primarily due to higher interest rates on long-term debt, contributing to a net loss of $412 million.

name: International Expansion Regulatory Risk
description: As the company introduces new products and services and expands into new markets, it expects to become subject to additional regulations, restrictions, and licensing requirements across multiple jurisdictions.

name: International Growth and Expansion
description: The company faces risks related to its international growth, expansion, and the management of that growth.

name: International Operations Risk
description: International operations expose the company to risks including changes in local political, economic, regulatory, tax, social, and labor conditions; restrictions on foreign ownership and investments; stringent foreign exchange controls; import and export requirements; tariffs; trade disputes; longer payment cycles; increased credit risk; higher payment fraud; developing foreign laws; uncertainty regarding liability for services and content; and different employee/employer relationships.

name: International Trade Restrictions
description: Restrictions on international trade, such as tariffs and other controls on imports or exports of goods, technology or data, can materially adversely affect the Company's operations and supply chain and limit the Company's ability to offer and distribute its products and services to customers.

name: Inventory Excess Risk
description: Excess supply of NVIDIA Ampere architecture Gaming and Data Center products relative to demand expectations, particularly for expected demand in China, resulted in $2.17 billion of inventory charges.

name: Inventory Management Risk
description: Inventory management is subject to risks arising from variability in customer demand and in fulfillment throughput and productivity, which can adversely affect operations.

name: Inventory Obsolescence Risk
description: The Company records write-downs for product and component inventories that have become obsolete or exceed anticipated demand, or for which cost exceeds net realizable value.

name: Inventory Provision Risk
description: There is a risk of incurring inventory provisions if inventory or supply commitments are misaligned with demand. Supply, including inventory on hand, purchase obligations, and prepaid supply agreements, has grown significantly.

name: Inventory Risk
description: The company faces the risk of holding excess inventory due to unexpected changes in demand, cancellation or deferral of customer purchase orders, improper demand estimation, or increases in competitive product demand.

name: Inventory Turnover Variability
description: Variability in inventory turnover is affected by numerous factors including product mix, mix of sales by third-party sellers, in-stock inventory availability, supply chain disruptions and resulting vendor lead times, investment in new geographies and product lines, and use of third-party fulfillment providers.

name: Inventory Valuation Adjustments
description: Costs associated with inventory valuation adjustments are included in cost of revenue, posing a financial risk if adjustments are significant.

name: Inventory Valuation Risk
description: Inventories are valued using the first-in first-out method at the lower of cost and net realizable value, and assumptions about future disposition of inventory are inherently uncertain. For every 1% of additional inventory valuation allowance as of December 31, 2022, an additional cost of sales of approximately $390 million would have been recorded.

name: Inventory Write-Downs and Purchase Commitment Cancellation
description: The Company is exposed to the risk of inventory write-downs for product and component inventories that have become obsolete or exceed anticipated demand, as well as purchase commitment cancellation risk.

name: Inventory and Purchase Commitment Risk
description: Non-cancellable and non-returnable purchasing commitments placed well in advance of historical lead times create the risk of excess inventory, cancellation penalties, impairments, and inventory write-downs.

name: Inventory and Staffing Optimization Risk
description: Failure to optimize inventory or staffing in fulfillment network increases net shipping cost. Regional labor market and global supply chain constraints increase payroll costs and make it difficult to hire, train, and deploy sufficient staff.

name: Inverse Condemnation Liability
description: Inverse condemnation imposes strict liability, including liability for attorneys' fees, for damages resulting from the design, construction, and maintenance of utility facilities such as electric transmission lines. Plaintiffs assert this doctrine in lawsuits related to certain wildfires in the Utility's service area.

name: Investigations and Litigation Risk
description: Other companies in the technology industry with which the Company has commercial relationships are or may become subject to investigations and litigation. If resolved against those other companies, such proceedings could materially adversely affect the Company's commercial relationships, business, results of operations, and financial condition.

name: Investment Portfolio Risk
description: Investment portfolio is subject to general credit, liquidity, market, and interest rate risks, which may be exacerbated by market downturns or a downgrade of the U.S. government credit rating, potentially requiring impairment charges.

name: Investment Return Risk
description: Significant investments in products and services may not achieve expected returns. Continued investments in research, development, and marketing may not generate sufficient revenue growth.

name: Investment Volatility Risk
description: Holding investments in publicly traded companies could create volatility in results and may generate losses up to the value of the investment.

name: Investment and Acquisition Risks
description: Investments in new business strategies or acquisitions may involve significant risks, including management distraction, greater-than-expected liabilities and expenses, economic, political, legal, and regulatory challenges, inadequate return on capital, and potential asset impairment or write-offs. There is also a risk of failure to obtain required regulatory approvals.

name: IoT Security, Privacy, and Execution Risk
description: IoT products and services may contain defects, have limited update capabilities, collect large amounts of data, and affect personal health and safety, leading to legal claims or enforcement actions.

name: Israel Operations Risk
description: Other countries have restricted, and may continue to restrict, business with the State of Israel through measures such as economic boycotts, which poses a risk to the company's engineering, sales support, and manufacturing operations located there.

name: Jurisdictional Regulatory Uncertainty Risk
description: There may be uncertainty whether the Singapore-based service is subject only to Singapore law or also to other local laws, and whether such local laws might require a payment processor to be licensed as a payments service, bank, or financial institution.

name: Key Metrics Accuracy Risk
description: Real or perceived inaccuracies in key metrics, which are calculated using internal company data, may harm PayPal's reputation and negatively affect its business.

name: Key Personnel Retention Risk
description: The loss of key personnel, or the inability to attract, hire, develop, motivate, and retain highly qualified and diverse talent in both remote and in-office environments, could harm the company's overall business and results of operations.

name: Labor Market Competition
description: A highly competitive labor market for skilled workers and leaders, combined with cost inflation, the effects of the COVID-19 pandemic, and varying workforce participation rates, creates significant challenges in attracting and retaining qualified employees.

name: Lease Expiry
description: The Company's head office lease is due to expire in fiscal 2024 and the Company has not yet finalized a renewal of the current lease terms.

name: Legacy System Support Risk
description: Customers expect continued support for legacy systems that may not incorporate the strongest current security advances or standards, making it difficult for the company to discontinue support for less secure products.

name: Legal Claims and Litigation
description: The company faces legal actions for defamation, negligence, breaches of contract, copyright or trademark infringement, unfair competition, unlawful activity, tort, fraud, or other theories based on the nature and content of information published or linked to.

name: Legal Liabilities Risk
description: Accrued aggregate legal liabilities of $617 million as of June 30, 2023, with adverse outcomes potentially reaching approximately $600 million beyond recorded amounts.

name: Legal Proceedings and Claims
description: Outcomes of legal proceedings and claims may include significant monetary damages or injunctive relief and could have a material adverse impact on operating results.

name: Legal and Other Contingencies
description: The Company is subject to various legal proceedings and claims that arise in the ordinary course of business, the outcomes of which are inherently uncertain. Resolution of legal matters in a manner inconsistent with management's expectations could have a material impact on the Company's financial condition and operating results.

name: Legal and Regulatory Compliance Risks
description: The Company's business, results of operations, and financial condition could be adversely impacted by unfavorable results of legal proceedings or government investigations.

name: Legal and Regulatory Proceedings
description: The company faces risks arising from legal or regulatory proceedings to which it is a party, as referenced in the Risk Factors and Commitments and Contingencies disclosures.

name: Legal and Regulatory Requirements Risk
description: Existing and increasing legal and regulatory requirements, spanning areas such as user privacy, telecommunications, data storage and protection, advertising, and online content, could adversely affect results of operations.

name: Legislative and Regulatory Developments
description: PG&E Corporation and the Utility could be materially affected by legislative and regulatory developments, as their operations are subject to extensive federal, state, and local laws, regulations, and orders. Compliance with these requirements results in significant capital, operating, and other costs.

name: License Maintenance and Renewal Risk
description: Maintaining and renewing licenses, certifications, and approvals involves substantial costs and potential product and operational changes. The company could be subject to enforcement actions, fines, and litigation if found to violate any requirements.

name: License and Permit Risk
description: The Utility's asset retirement obligation could be materially impacted if required federal and state licenses, permits, and approvals for Diablo Canyon extended operations are not received.

name: Limited Customer Technical Sophistication
description: Customers of widely varied size and technical sophistication may have limited capabilities and resources to adopt and implement state-of-the-art cybersecurity practices and technologies.

name: Limited Suppliers and Component Shortages
description: Limited suppliers for certain device and datacenter components may lead to delays or unavailability of components, resulting in reduced sales, inadequate datacenter capacity, increased cost of revenue, and supply chain disruptions.

name: Liquidity Risk from Loan Portfolio Growth
description: Growth in the portfolio of loan receivables increases liquidity needs, and any inability to meet those liquidity needs could adversely affect the business.

name: Liquidity and Capital Market Access Risk
description: The Utility's ability to fund operations, finance capital expenditures, make scheduled principal and interest payments, and make distributions depends on its operating cash flows and continued access to capital and credit markets.

name: Liquidity and Financing Risk
description: There can be no assurance that additional credit lines or financing instruments will be available in amounts or on terms acceptable to the company.

name: Litigation Contingencies
description: PG&E Corporation and the Utility face significant contingencies arising from their operations, including enforcement and litigation matters that could have a material effect on financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows.

name: Litigation Settlement Risk
description: The Company may enter into agreements or other arrangements to settle litigation, which can significantly increase its cost of sales and operating expenses. Such settlements may also require the Company to change its business practices and limit its ability to offer certain products and services.

name: Litigation and Government Investigations
description: The company faces risks from the outcomes of claims, litigation, government investigations, and other proceedings.

name: Litigation and Regulatory Proceedings
description: Business is exposed to the risks associated with litigation, investigations and regulatory proceedings.

name: Local Partner Dependency Risk
description: In certain markets, the company may need to rely on local banks or other partners to process payments and conduct foreign currency exchange transactions, and local regulators may use their authority over such local partners to prohibit, restrict, or limit operations.

name: Local RA Procurement Failure
description: The Utility, as CPE, risks failing to procure resources to meet local RA requirements, deferring to CAISO backstop mechanisms when bid costs are deemed unreasonably high.

name: Loss Contingencies Risk
description: The company is regularly subject to claims, suits, government investigations, and other proceedings involving competition and antitrust, intellectual property, privacy, indirect taxes, labor and employment, and commercial disputes. Certain matters include speculative claims for substantial or indeterminate amounts of damages.

name: Loss Contingency Estimation Risk
description: The process for estimating wildfire-related and other liabilities requires management to exercise significant judgment based on assumptions and subjective factors. Actual results may differ materially from estimates and assumptions.

name: Loss of Key Senior Management and Personnel
description: The company depends on senior management and other key personnel, including the President and CEO, and carries no key person life insurance. Intense competition for qualified personnel, particularly software engineers, computer scientists, and other technical staff in the technology industry, poses a significant retention and recruitment risk.

name: Lost Generation Value Disallowance Risk
description: The CPUC may disallow the value of lost generation due to unplanned outages at utility-owned generation facilities.

name: Macroeconomic Conditions
description: Macroeconomic factors including inflation, increased interest rates, significant capital market volatility, the prolonged COVID-19 pandemic, global supply chain constraints, and global economic and geopolitical developments have direct and indirect impacts on results of operations.

name: Macroeconomic Conditions & COVID-19 Disruptions
description: Global macro-economic conditions and COVID-19 related disruptions in China weighed on gaming demand, leading to lower sell-in to partners and reduced channel inventory levels.

name: Macroeconomic Environment Risk
description: The company faces broader macroeconomic risks including COVID-19 uncertainty, the Russia-Ukraine conflict, supply chain shortages, global recession, and elevated inflation and interest rates. These conditions could lead to lower consumer spending, merchant and consumer bankruptcies, higher credit losses, and adverse foreign currency exchange fluctuations.

name: Macroeconomic Forecast Risk
description: A significant change in forecasted macroeconomic factors such as unemployment rates, retail e-commerce sales, and household disposable income could result in a material change in allowances.

name: Macroeconomic Uncertainty
description: Macroeconomic uncertainty affects the allowances maintained for merchant and consumer portfolios, including qualitative adjustments and changes to acceptable risk parameters.

name: Macroeconomic Volatility
description: Continued macroeconomic volatility and uncertainty around the financial health of merchant borrowers, including uncertainty around the effectiveness of loan modification programs, pose ongoing risks to the business.

name: Macroeconomic and COVID-19 Disruption Risk
description: Global macro-economic conditions and COVID-19 related disruptions in China weighed on gaming demand, contributing to lower revenue.

name: Macroeconomic and Government Policy Risk
description: Government fiscal and tax policies, trade relationships, tariffs, restrictive actions, government shutdowns, austerity programs, and other adverse macroeconomic developments could materially reduce demand for PayPal's products and services, including the volume and size of transactions on its payments platform.

name: Macroeconomic and Industry Risks
description: The Company's operations and performance depend significantly on global and regional economic conditions. Adverse macroeconomic conditions, including slow growth or recession, high unemployment, inflation, tighter credit, higher interest rates, and currency fluctuations, can adversely impact consumer confidence and spending and materially adversely affect demand for the Company's products and services.

name: Malicious Cyber Attacks
description: The Company experiences malicious attacks and other attempts to gain unauthorized access to its systems on a regular basis. These attacks seek to compromise the confidentiality, integrity or availability of confidential information or disrupt normal business operations, and can impair the Company's ability to attract and retain customers, impact stock price, materially damage commercial relationships, and expose the Company to litigation or government investigations.

name: Mandatory R&D Capitalization (TCJA)
description: Beginning in FY2023, the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act requires taxpayers to capitalize R&D expenditures and amortize domestic expenditures over five years and foreign expenditures over fifteen years, creating an adverse cash flow impact.

name: Manufacturing Disruption Risk
description: Manufacturing, assembly, and other processes may be disrupted by facility closures related to heat waves, natural disasters, or electricity conservation efforts, as well as by low manufacturing yields resulting from product design failures or foundry process technology issues.

name: Manufacturing Lead Time Risk
description: Long manufacturing lead times, in some cases extending beyond twelve months for some products, requiring non-cancellable inventory orders, premium payments, and deposits to secure future supply and capacity.

name: Manufacturing and Logistics Disruption
description: Manufacturing or logistics in outsourced locations or in transit to final destinations can be disrupted by natural and man-made disasters, IT system failures, commercial disputes, armed conflict, or economic, labor, environmental, public health, political, or international trade issues.

name: Market Expansion Risk
description: Growth depends on the ability to reach new users in new markets, including frontline workers, small and medium businesses, and growth markets.

name: Market Risk
description: The company faces potential economic losses on market risk sensitive instruments arising from adverse changes in market factors such as interest rates, foreign currency exchange rates, and equity investment values.

name: Market Risk - Interest Rates and Foreign Exchange
description: The Company is exposed to economic risk from interest rates and foreign exchange rate fluctuations.

name: Market Value Depression Risk
description: Substantial sales of stock by existing stockholders, including the Fire Victim Trust, could depress the market value of PG&E Corporation's common stock and make it more difficult to sell equity or equity-linked securities in the future.

name: Material Weakness in Internal Controls
description: Identification of material weaknesses in internal controls may cause investors to lose confidence, stock price decline, restatement of financials, restricted capital market access, or regulatory sanctions.

name: Media, Political, and Regulatory Scrutiny
description: The media, political, and regulatory scrutiny Amazon faces, which may continue to increase, amplifies regulatory and litigation risks.

name: Merchant Financial Condition Risk
description: Failure to effectively monitor and evaluate the financial condition of merchants may expose PayPal to losses, particularly in the event of merchant bankruptcy, insolvency, or business failure where goods or services are sold in advance of delivery.

name: Merchant Financial Health Risk
description: The company faces uncertainty around the financial health of merchant borrowers and the effectiveness of loan modification programs made available to merchants.

name: Merchant Financing Legal Challenge Risk
description: The ability to receive the benefit of U.S. merchant financing offerings and certain installment loan products may be subject to legal challenge regarding federal preemption of state usury laws, potentially impacting PayPal's merchant financing and installment products.

name: Mid-Term Reliability Risk
description: Incremental electric system reliability needs between 2024 and 2026 due to the pending retirement of once-through-cooling natural gas plants in Southern California and the possible retirement of Diablo Canyon, requiring at least 11,500 MW of additional net qualifying capacity to be procured by LSEs.

name: Minimum Return Threshold Risk
description: In the event the overall return on PayPal branded credit programs funded by the partner institution does not meet a minimum rate of return in a particular quarter, PayPal's revenues may be negatively impacted.

name: Minority Market Share
description: The Company holds a minority market share in the global smartphone, personal computer, and tablet markets, facing substantial competition from companies with significant technical, marketing, and distribution resources as well as established supplier relationships.

name: Motor Vehicle or Aviation Incident
description: A motor vehicle or aviation incident involving a Utility vehicle or aircraft resulting in serious injuries, fatalities, property damage, or other consequences.

name: Municipalization / Eminent Domain Risk
description: Jurisdictions may attempt to acquire the Utility's assets through eminent domain, and the City and County of San Francisco has submitted a petition with the CPUC seeking a valuation of the Utility's electric assets in San Francisco with expressed intent to acquire them.

name: Municipalization Risk
description: Municipalities could seek to acquire Utility assets through eminent domain or municipalization proceedings, which would reduce the Utility's rate base, revenues, and profitability while materially affecting its financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows.

name: Municipalization and State Takeover Risk
description: The Utility's business profile and financial results could be adversely affected by calls for municipalization of part or all of its businesses, actions by municipalities to acquire electric assets, or calls for state intervention including a potential state takeover.

name: NEM Successor Tariff Subsidy Reduction
description: New customers interconnecting under the successor tariff face reduced subsidies: about 60% reduction for standalone solar, about 45% for solar-paired storage, and about 35% for new commercial customers.

name: NEM Tariff Change Risk
description: The CPUC final decision will reduce the NEM subsidy by reducing the bill credits for exported energy to avoided cost levels for new customers interconnecting under the successor tariff, potentially impacting customer-generators and the Utility's business.

name: NRC License Renewal Risk
description: If NRC license renewal or approval from California state agencies is not received, the Utility would retire Unit 1 in 2024 and Unit 2 in 2025. The Utility has requested the NRC resume review of a license renewal application that was voluntarily withdrawn in 2018.

name: NRC License Renewal Uncertainty
description: The NRC staff declined to resume review of the previously withdrawn license renewal application and directed the Utility to submit a new application, creating uncertainty about the continuation of operations at Diablo Canyon.

name: NRC Regulatory Compliance Cost Risk
description: The Utility has incurred and may continue to incur substantial costs to comply with NRC regulations and orders. If unable to recover these costs, or if it cannot comply in a feasible and economic manner, the Utility may voluntarily or be ordered to cease operations at Diablo Canyon, potentially incurring material charges.

name: Nation-State Cyber Attack Risk
description: Sophisticated threat actors, including nation-states and nation-state-supported groups, engage in cyber-attacks especially during geopolitical conflicts and times of war, potentially disrupting the company's ability to provide services and products.

name: Natural Disasters and Catastrophic Events
description: Increasing frequency and intensity of weather events related to climate change, seismic activity in San Francisco Bay Area, could lead to disruption or failure of systems or operations, resulting in significant losses and recovery costs.

name: Natural Disasters and Climate Change
description: Many of the Company's operations and facilities are in locations prone to earthquakes and other natural disasters. Global climate change is resulting in certain types of natural disasters occurring more frequently or with more intense effects, making it difficult to manufacture and deliver products.

name: Natural Gas Cost Recovery Risk
description: Inability by the Utility to recover through rates its investments into the natural gas system while still ensuring gas system safety and reliability could materially affect the Utility's financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows.

name: Natural Gas Overpressure Event Risk
description: An overpressure event on natural gas facilities due to equipment failure, incorrect operating procedures, or fabrication defects can result in pipeline failure and uncontained natural gas flow.

name: Natural Gas Price Risk
description: The cost of natural gas increased in 2022 compared to 2021 due to higher customer demand and elevated market prices.

name: Natural Gas Price Volatility
description: Natural gas costs may increase due to factors including higher customer demand, below-normal winter temperatures, prolonged drought conditions, lower hydro-electric generation output, lower natural gas storage levels, and regional pipeline constraints.

name: Natural Gas Stranded Assets Risk
description: Various jurisdictions within California have enacted prohibitions or restrictions on natural gas use, which could reduce the gas customer base, diminish need for gas infrastructure, and cause certain gas assets to no longer be 'used and useful,' potentially stranding substantial investment value and reducing rate recovery.

name: Natural Gas Supply Concentration Risk
description: The Utility's largest individual supplier represented approximately 47% of the total natural gas volume purchased during 2022, creating significant supplier concentration risk.

name: Natural and Human-Caused Disaster Risk
description: Natural or human-caused disasters, including public health crises, extreme weather, geopolitical events, security issues, and labor or trade disputes, could disrupt business operations.

name: Net Energy Metering (NEM) Cost Shifts
description: Increasing levels of self-generation by customers and enrollment in NEM shifts costs to non-NEM customers, increasing rate pressure on remaining customers.

name: Net Operating Loss Carryforward Limitation Risk
description: Limitations on PG&E Corporation's ability to utilize net operating loss carryforwards or other tax attributes could cause U.S. federal and California income taxes to be paid earlier than otherwise required. Such limitations could also cause carryforwards to expire unused, reducing or eliminating their benefit.

name: Network Operator Fees
description: Operators of the networks between customers and the company's online stores may successfully charge fees to grant customers unimpaired and unconstrained access to online services, which could increase costs and affect competitiveness.

name: Network Rule Violations and Fines
description: PayPal may be subject to fines and penalties assessed by networks resulting from rule violations by PayPal or its merchants. Networks have alleged that various aspects of PayPal's business model violate their rules, potentially resulting in significant fines, penalties, damages, or required changes in business practices.

name: New Product Adoption Risk
description: If customers do not perceive latest offerings as providing significant new functionality or value, they may reduce purchases of new software and hardware products or upgrades, unfavorably affecting revenue.

name: New Use Case Demand Uncertainty
description: NVIDIA builds technology and products for use cases and applications that may be new or may not yet exist. Demand estimates for these use cases and applications can be incorrect and create volatility in revenue or supply levels.

name: Non-Marketable Equity Securities Impairment Risk
description: Non-marketable equity securities accounted for under the Measurement Alternative are reviewed for impairment when events and circumstances indicate a decline in fair value below carrying value, based on factors such as the investees' financial condition, business outlook, industry performance, and regulatory, economic, or technological environment.

name: Non-cancellable Inventory Orders
description: NVIDIA has placed non-cancellable inventory orders for certain product components in advance of historical lead times and has paid premiums and provided deposits to secure future supply and capacity, creating financial exposure if demand does not materialize.

name: Nuclear Accident Risk
description: A nuclear accident, seismic activity, natural disaster, or terrorist act could cause the release of radioactive materials, posing significant safety and liability risks.

name: Nuclear Decommissioning Cost Risk
description: The Utility has an obligation to decommission its electricity generation facilities including nuclear facilities. If actual decommissioning costs exceed estimated costs, financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows could be materially affected.

name: Nuclear Fuel Disposal
description: The DOE has been unable to meet its contractual obligation to dispose of nuclear waste from the Utility's two nuclear generating units at Diablo Canyon and the retired nuclear facility at Humboldt Bay, requiring the Utility to construct interim dry cask storage facilities onsite.

name: Nuclear Generation Facilities Risk
description: The operation and decommissioning of the Utility's nuclear generation facilities expose it to potentially significant liabilities from environmental, health, and financial risks. The Utility may not be able to fully recover its costs if regulatory requirements or operating conditions change or the facilities cease operations before the licenses expire.

name: Nuclear Generation Liability Risk
description: Operation of nuclear generation facilities exposes the Utility to significant liabilities from environmental, health, and financial risks, including risks relating to operation of Diablo Canyon nuclear generation units, storage, handling, and disposal of spent nuclear fuel, and release of radioactive materials caused by nuclear accident, seismic activity, natural disaster, or terrorist act.

name: Nuclear Insurance Coverage Risk
description: If the Utility incurs losses not covered by insurance or exceeding available insurance coverage, such losses could have a material effect on financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows. The Utility may be required under federal law to pay up to $275 million per nuclear incident.

name: Nuclear Operations Outage Risk
description: Diablo Canyon Unit 2 experienced five outages between July 2020 and April 2021 due to excessive vibrations within the main generator, adversely affecting capacity factors and generation availability.

name: Nuclear Opposition and Public Concern Risk
description: The Utility faces public concern about nuclear generation safety. Nuclear opposition groups file petitions at the NRC challenging its actions. Public pressure could lead to adverse changes in legislation, regulations, or orders, potentially causing operations at Diablo Canyon to cease before licenses expire in 2024 and 2025.

name: Nuclear Regulatory Risk
description: NRC has authority to impose fines or force a shutdown of a nuclear plant in the event of non-compliance. NRC safety and security requirements have necessitated substantial capital expenditures at Diablo Canyon.

name: OEM Channel Inventory Levels
description: Changes in inventory levels in the OEM channel impact Windows OEM revenue.

name: OEM Partner Competition Compliance Risk
description: The company both cooperates and competes with OEM partners, creating a risk of non-compliance with competition rules, with increasing regulatory scrutiny in this area.

name: Online Advertising and Technology Risk
description: New and existing technologies or industry trends may restrict online advertising or affect ability to customize advertising or tailor product and service offerings.

name: Open Source Competition
description: Some companies compete by modifying and distributing open source software at little or no cost, using open source AI models, without bearing the full costs of research and development. Some open source products mimic the features and functionality of Microsoft products.

name: Open Source Licensing Risk
