description: Increasing engagement with open source software may cause the company to license its intellectual property rights broadly in certain situations.

name: Open Source Software Vulnerabilities
description: The company increasingly incorporates open source software into its products, which may contain vulnerabilities that make those products susceptible to cyberattacks.

name: Operating Cost Control Risk
description: Under cost-of-service ratemaking, the Utility's earnings depend on its ability to manage costs within authorized amounts, including a goal of increasing capital investments while reducing non-fuel operating and maintenance costs by two percent per year through implementation of the Lean operating system.

name: Operating Results Fluctuation
description: Operating results may fluctuate as a result of a number of factors, many outside of the company's control, including ability to attract and retain users, monetization of traffic, revenue fluctuations from property/platform/geographical mix, operating costs, capital expenditures, and seasonality and cyclicality of advertising spending.

name: Operational Disruption Risk
description: The Company could be subject to unexpected developments, such as lower-than-anticipated demand, issues with new product or service introductions, information technology system failures or network disruptions, or failure of one of the Company's logistics, components supply, or manufacturing partners.

name: Operational Disruptions
description: Operations could be harmed and costs could increase if manufacturing, logistics, or other operations are disrupted due to natural disasters, high heat events, water shortages, IT system failures, military actions, or economic, business, labor, environmental, public health, or political issues.

name: Operational Expansion Risk
description: Rapid expansion places a significant strain on management, operational, financial, and other resources.

name: Operational Inefficiency Risk
description: Inefficiencies in process and workflow that the company aims to eliminate through Lean deployment and waste elimination strategies.

name: Operational Outages and Infrastructure Failures
description: Microsoft may experience excessive outages, data losses, and disruptions of online services if it fails to maintain an adequate operations infrastructure. Increasing user traffic, growth in services, and product complexity demand more computing power.

name: Operations and IT Risk
description: The Utility faces operational and IT risks including hazardous electricity and natural gas operations, changes in the electric power and gas industries, cyber incidents, nuclear generation facility operation and decommissioning, and challenges in attracting and retaining specialty personnel.

name: Operator or Human Error
description: Operator or other human error leading to operational failures or safety incidents.

name: Outsourcing Partner Financial Risk
description: If outsourcing partners or suppliers experience severe financial problems or other disruptions, supply may be reduced or terminated, and the recoverability of manufacturing process equipment or prepayments may be negatively impacted.

name: Outsourcing and Supply Chain Dependency
description: Substantially all manufacturing is performed by outsourcing partners located primarily in China mainland, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, and Vietnam, with significant concentration among a small number of partners often operating in single locations. This outsourcing reduces direct control over production and distribution, which may adversely affect the quality or quantity of products manufactured.

name: Overpressure and Outage Risk
description: Risk of increasing pressure on gas systems leading to outages and overpressure events, mitigated by automatic safety devices.

name: Ownership Change Risk
description: Under Section 382 of the IRC, if PG&E Corporation undergoes an ownership change, net operating loss carryforwards and other tax attributes may be subject to certain limitations. An ownership change occurs if aggregate stock ownership of certain shareholders increases by more than 50% over their lowest percentage ownership during the testing period.

name: Ownership Limitation Risk
description: PG&E Corporation's Amended Articles impose a 4.75% ownership limitation on percentage stock ownership, with shares held by the Fire Victim Trust, the Utility, and ShareCo excluded from the outstanding share count, resulting in an effective ownership limitation of approximately 3.46% as of February 16, 2023.

name: Ownership Restrictions Complexity
description: The application of Ownership Restrictions under PG&E Corporation's Amended Articles of Incorporation may be determined on the basis of shares outstanding that could differ materially from shares reported as outstanding, due to the grantor trust election for the Fire Victim Trust.

name: Ownership Restrictions on Equity Securities
description: The Amended Articles impose restrictions on the transferability and ownership of PG&E Corporation common stock, preferred stock, and other Equity Securities to reduce the possibility of an equity ownership shift that could limit the ability to utilize net operating loss carryforwards and other tax attributes. Any person or entity acquiring 4.75% or more of outstanding Equity Securities may be in violation of these restrictions, and transferees receiving Equity Securities in violation will not be recognized as shareholders.

name: PC Shipments Dependency
description: Growth in Devices is dependent on total PC shipments and the ability to attract new customers.

name: PISA Regulation Risk
description: PayPal (Europe) is subject to regulation by the ECB under the oversight framework for electronic payment instruments, schemes and arrangements (PISA), which may lead to increased compliance obligations and costs.

name: PRC Regulatory Risk
description: The People's Republic of China regulates Amazon's businesses through laws and license requirements that restrict foreign investment, Internet content, and the sale of media and other products and services.

name: PSPS Program Risk
description: The PSPS program has been subject to significant scrutiny and criticism. The Utility may be subject to mandated changes, regulatory fines, penalties, claims for damages, and reputational harm if PSPS is not executed in compliance with applicable rules and regulations.

name: PSPS and EPSS Reputational Risk
description: The PSPS and EPSS programs have had an adverse impact on the Utility's reputation with customers, regulators, and policymakers, and future events may further increase negative perceptions.

name: Partner Bank Dependency Risk
description: PayPal relies on unaffiliated financial institutions to provide PayPal and Venmo branded consumer credit and merchant financing offerings. Any termination or interruption in a partner bank's ability or willingness to lend could interrupt or limit PayPal's ability to offer these credit products.

name: Partner Institution Credit Risk
description: Consumers with outstanding loans through the partner institution may experience financial hardships, resulting in losses recognized by that institution. Such losses may reduce PayPal's revenue share in future periods.

name: Partner Relationship Risks
description: PayPal often partners with other businesses, and competition for these partner relationships is intense. There is no assurance that PayPal will be able to continue to establish, grow, or maintain these partner relationships, which are important to its business.

name: Partner and Customer Purchasing Pattern Risk
description: Partners or customers may develop their own solutions, purchase from competitors, discontinue sales, or alter purchasing patterns, impacting revenues. Most sales are on a purchase order basis allowing cancellation with little or no notice.

name: Patent Infringement Claims Risk
description: The Company has faced and continues to face a significant number of patent claims relating to its cellular-enabled products. Technology and other patent-holding companies frequently assert their patents and seek royalties and often enter into litigation based on allegations of patent infringement or other violations of intellectual property rights.

name: Pay Equity Gap
description: Unadjusted median pay gaps exist between women and men and between racial/ethnic minorities and white employees, which may widen if representation at senior levels does not improve.

name: Payment Card Acceptance Risk
description: If losses related to payment card transactions become excessive, PayPal could lose the ability to accept payment cards, which would negatively impact its business.

name: Payment Card Breach Liability
description: Under payment card network rules and contracts with payment processors, if there is a breach of payment card information, PayPal could be liable to payment card issuing banks including cost of issuing new cards and related expenses.

name: Payment Card Compliance Risk
description: The company is subject to payment card association operating rules, including data security rules, certification requirements, and rules governing electronic funds transfers. Failure to comply could result in liability for card-issuing banks' costs, fines, higher transaction fees, and loss of the ability to accept credit and debit card payments.

name: Payment Card Data Risk
description: Payment card data is subject to additional requirements under payment card rules and obligations, exposing the Company to compliance risk.

name: Payment Card Network Fee Changes
description: Changes to payment card network or bank fees, rules, or practices could harm PayPal's business. Networks have increased fees and assessments for transactions, including special fees for digital wallet transactions. Increases in interchange fees or assessments could make PayPal's pricing less competitive, increase operating costs, and reduce operating income.

name: Payment Funding Mix Risk
description: Changes in how consumers fund their PayPal transactions, particularly increased use of payment cards which carry higher fees, could harm financial performance and business.

name: Payment Type Acceptance Risk
description: Regulatory changes or difficulty complying with rules could limit PayPal's ability to accept certain payment types such as debit or credit cards, materially and adversely affecting business.

name: Payments Regulation Risk
description: Failure to comply with applicable laws or regulations required to maintain money transmitter licenses could result in liability, additional restrictions, forced cessation of business with residents of certain states or territories, or requirement to obtain additional licenses.

name: Payments-Related Risks
description: The company accepts payments via credit cards, debit cards, gift cards, and other methods, and is subject to related regulations, compliance requirements, fraud risks, interchange fees, and reliance on third parties for payment processing services.

name: Pension and Postretirement Benefit Obligation Risk
description: Significant differences in actual experience, plan changes or amendments, or changes in actuarial assumptions may materially affect recorded pension and other postretirement benefit obligations and future plan expenses.

name: Permitting and Construction Risk
description: Timing of expenditures and cost recovery may be affected by permitting requirements, delays, construction schedules, availability of labor, equipment and materials, financing, legal and regulatory approvals, community protests, weather, and other unforeseen conditions.

name: Personal Data Regulations
description: Laws and regulations relating to the handling of personal data may impede the adoption of services or result in increased costs, legal claims, fines, or reputational damage. Legal requirements relating to the collection, storage, handling, and transfer of personal data continue to evolve globally.

name: Personnel Management Risk
description: The inability to hire, train, retain, and manage sufficient required personnel may limit international growth.

name: Personnel Retention Risk at Diablo Canyon
description: If Diablo Canyon is retired by 2025, the Utility will incur costs for employee retention and retraining programs to maintain adequate staffing levels. There is no assurance the Utility will be successful in retaining highly skilled personnel under its employee programs.

name: Personnel and Labor Risk
description: Competition for technical staff, potential loss of executive officers, failure to hire, train, or retain qualified personnel, labor union efforts, and changes in laws affecting employees and contractors could harm business operations and reputation.

name: Physical Climate Change Risk
description: The Utility faces increasing physical risks from climate change, including extreme weather and related emergencies that threaten its infrastructure, assets, and operations, requiring substantial investments to build resilience.

name: Piracy
description: Piracy of the Windows operating system negatively impacts Windows OEM revenue and overall business performance.

name: Platform Competition
description: Users are increasingly turning to devices that compete with personal computers, making it more difficult to attract application developers to PC operating system platforms. Competing platforms have content and application marketplaces with scale and significant installed bases.

name: Platform Downtime and Resiliency Risk
description: The payments platform faces risk of outages or catastrophic occurrences that could disrupt operations, mitigated by multiple layers of protection for business continuity and system redundancy.

name: Platform Ecosystem Competition
description: Microsoft faces significant competition from firms that provide competing platforms, including vertically-integrated models where a single firm controls software and hardware elements.

name: Political and Global Disruption Risks
description: Political events, trade and other international disputes, war, terrorism, natural disasters, public health issues, industrial accidents and other business interruptions can harm or disrupt international commerce and the global economy, and could have a material adverse effect on the Company and its customers, suppliers, contract manufacturers, and logistics providers.

name: Post-Sale Monetization Risk
description: Microsoft may not achieve sufficient user engagement in post-sale monetization features such as Microsoft Edge, Bing, and Bing Chat, limiting associated revenue opportunities.

name: Privacy Policy Compliance Risk
description: Any failure by the Company to comply with its public statements or with federal, state or international privacy or data protection laws and regulations could result in inquiries or proceedings against the Company by governmental entities or others, including ongoing audit requirements and significant legal liability.

name: Privacy and Data Regulation Risk
description: The company is subject to laws and regulations regarding privacy, data use, data protection, data security, data localization, network security, consumer protection, payments, advertising, and restrictions on pricing or discounts.

name: Privacy and Data Security Risk - CCPA/CPRA
description: Failure to comply with the CCPA and CPRA could result in litigation, audits, and the imposition of material fines. Personal information collected could become compromised due to cyber incidents, human error, data misappropriation, or third-party failures, subjecting the companies to financial liability.

name: Procurement Cost Disallowance
description: The CPUC may disallow costs associated with the CPE function that were not incurred in compliance with the CPUC's decisions and guidance.

name: Product Defect Liability Risk
description: The Company generally remains responsible to consumers for warranty and out-of-warranty service in the event of product defects, and experiences unanticipated product defect liabilities from time to time.

name: Product Defect Risk
description: Hardware and software products are complex and may contain defects, security vulnerabilities, or experience failures due to issues in design, fabrication, packaging, materials, or system integration, potentially leading to significant warranty, support, repair, or replacement costs.

name: Product Defects and Quality Issues
description: Highly complex devices and software products may have defects in design, manufacture, or associated software, leading to significant expenses, lost revenue, and reputational harm as a result of recalls, safety alerts, or product liability claims.

name: Product Defects and Warranty Liability
description: Defects in third-party components embedded in products such as Data Center products can lead to warranty liabilities, product recalls, indemnification obligations, and significant costs. Business liability insurance may be inadequate to cover such exposures.

name: Product Imitation by Competitors
description: Competitors imitate the Company's product features and applications, or collaborate to offer solutions that are more competitive than those they currently provide.

name: Product Liability Claims
description: Some products sold or manufactured by the company expose it to product liability or food safety claims relating to personal injury, illness, death, or environmental or property damage, and third-party sellers also expose the company to such claims.

name: Product Transition Risk
description: Product transitions are complex as NVIDIA often ships both new and legacy architecture products simultaneously. Qualification time for new products, customers anticipating product transitions and channel partners reducing channel inventory can create reductions or volatility in revenue.

name: Product Warranty Obligations
description: The company provides for estimated costs of fulfilling obligations under hardware and software warranties. Hardware warranties generally cover parts and labor for periods ranging from 90 days to three years, while software warranties cover the cost of bug fixes such as security patches over the estimated life of the software.

name: Product and Service Security Threats
description: Security threats are a significant challenge as adversaries focus on popular operating systems, programs, and services. Vulnerabilities may persist after patches are released if customers have not installed updates or if attackers exploited them before patching.

name: Product and Service Transition Management
description: The Company must successfully manage frequent introductions and transitions of products and services to remain competitive and stimulate customer demand. Success depends on timely development, market acceptance, managing new technologies, production ramp-up, software availability, inventory management, and product quality.

name: Proliferation of Privacy Laws Risk
description: The continued proliferation of privacy laws in jurisdictions where PayPal operates is likely to result in a disparate array of privacy rules with unaligned or conflicting provisions, accountability requirements, individual rights, and enforcement powers, which may subject PayPal to increased regulatory scrutiny and business costs.

name: Protection Program Losses
description: PayPal incurs substantial losses from its protection programs as a result of customer disputes, and may not be able to fully recover those losses from merchants.

name: Public Health Issues
description: Major public health issues, including pandemics such as the COVID-19 pandemic, have adversely affected the Company due to their impact on the global economy, demand for consumer products, and disruptions in operations, supply chain and sales channels.

name: Purchase Commitment Cancellation Risk
description: The Company accrues necessary cancellation fee reserves for orders of excess products and components, and may not fully utilize firm purchase commitments.

name: Quality or Supply Problems
description: Hardware products such as Xbox consoles, Surface devices, and other devices are highly complex and can have defects in design, manufacture, or associated software, leading to significant expenses, lost revenue, and reputational harm.

name: Quarterly Net Sales Fluctuation Risk
description: The Company expects its quarterly net sales and results of operations to fluctuate. Profit margins vary across products, services, geographic segments and distribution channels and are subject to volatility and downward pressure due to pricing pressures, increased competition, supply shortages, foreign exchange fluctuations, and other factors.

name: R&D Investment Risk
description: The Company must make significant investments in R&D, but there is no assurance these investments will achieve expected returns or that the Company will successfully develop and market new products and services.

name: ROE Incentive Adder Refund Risk
description: The Utility could incur refund obligations of $1 million (TO16) and $8.5 million (TO17) if the FERC concludes the Utility should no longer receive the 50-basis point ROE incentive adder for CAISO participation.

name: ROE Rehearing Risk
description: The outcome of the ROE rehearing request may impact financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows of the Utility.

name: Ransomware Recovery Risk
description: The company's ability to recover from ransomware attacks may be limited if backups have been affected by the attack or if restoring from backups is delayed or not feasible.

name: Rapid Technological Change
description: The business is characterized by rapid change as well as new and disruptive technologies, requiring the company to rapidly deliver innovative products and technologies to the marketplace.

name: Rapidly Evolving Business Model and Stock Price Volatility
description: The company has a rapidly evolving business model. The trading price of common stock fluctuates significantly in response to changes in interest rates, industry trends, quarterly variations in operating results, stock market fluctuations, changes in financial estimates, capital structure changes, and transactions by major investors.

name: Rate Case Disallowance Risk
description: The CPUC may disallow capital spending from rate base, as seen in the 2015 GT&S rate case where $696 million was excluded and $120 million permanently disallowed.

name: Ratemaking and Cost Recovery Risk
description: The Utility's ratemaking and cost recovery proceedings may not authorize sufficient revenues, and actual costs could exceed authorized or forecasted amounts if the Utility is unable to manage its costs effectively.

name: Recessionary Fears
description: Recessionary fears may negatively impact customer demand and spending.

name: Reduced Gas Demand
description: Reduced load and increased costs to maintain the gas system could result in higher natural gas customer bills. Local city governments have passed ordinances restricting use of natural gas in new construction, affecting future demand.

name: Reduced Revenue from Market and Operational Events
description: The Utility may incur additional costs or receive reduced revenue due to changing market circumstances or unanticipated events such as wildfires, storms, earthquakes, accidents, increased customer self-generation, lower demand, or loss of customers to Community Choice Aggregators or Direct Access providers.

name: Refund Obligation Risk
description: Risk of refund obligations under TO16, TO17, and TO18 rate cases as determined by FERC and appellate court proceedings.

name: Regional Epidemics or Global Pandemic
description: The occurrence of regional epidemics or a global pandemic, such as COVID-19, may disrupt business operations and adversely affect financial results.

name: Regional Labor Market and Supply Chain Constraints
description: Regional labor market and global supply chain constraints may adversely impact the company's operations.

name: Regulated Payment Services Risk
description: In certain jurisdictions, the company provides regulated services that enable customers to hold account balances and transfer money to third parties, subjecting it to licensing, regulatory inspection, bonding, capital maintenance, consumer disclosure, and data requirements.

name: Regulatory Accounting Risk
description: If regulatory accounting did not apply, the Utility's future financial results could become more volatile due to differences in timing of expense or revenue recognition.

name: Regulatory Approval Risk
description: Difficulty or inability in obtaining governmental, regulatory approval or restrictions or other consents and approvals or financing for acquisitions.

name: Regulatory Asset Recovery Risk
description: Risk that regulatory assets may no longer be deemed probable of recovery, requiring them to be charged against income in the period that determination is made.

name: Regulatory Capital Requirements
description: Global cash balances must be maintained to satisfy regulatory capital requirements across the company's regulated subsidiaries, which limits the availability of cash for general corporate purposes.

name: Regulatory Challenges
description: Significant regulatory challenges prevented the completion of NVIDIA's acquisition of Arm Limited from SoftBank, resulting in the termination of the Share Purchase Agreement in February 2022.

name: Regulatory Changes to Interchange Fees
description: In some jurisdictions, government regulations have required payment card networks to reduce or cap interchange fees. Changes in interchange fee rates or their applicability to PayPal could adversely affect its competitive position, revenue from branded card programs, and require changes in business practices.

name: Regulatory Compliance Risk
description: Compliance with complex foreign and U.S. laws and regulations including internal control and disclosure rules, privacy and data protection requirements, anti-corruption laws such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and competition regulations increases cost of doing business. Violations could result in fines, penalties, criminal sanctions, and prohibitions on conducting business.

name: Regulatory Dependence
description: Decisions about expansions and maintenance of the transmission system can be influenced by decisions of the Utility's regulators and the CAISO.

name: Regulatory Developments Risk
description: Various regulatory agencies continue to examine and implement laws governing virtual currencies, identity theft, account management, cybersecurity, competition, marketing, and ESG performance, any of which may impact PayPal's business.

name: Regulatory Enforcement Risk
description: PayPal (Europe) may be subject to enforcement actions and significant fines if it violates applicable requirements. Compliance with applicable laws and regulations could become more costly and operationally difficult to manage.

name: Regulatory Non-Compliance Risk
description: OEIS found that the Utility failed to meet targets highly correlated with risk, failed to achieve critical stated objectives, and failed to sufficiently address risk on its system, resulting in a finding of non-substantial compliance with the WMP.

name: Regulatory Penalties
description: The Utility could face penalties, enforcement actions, or other adverse legal or regulatory consequences for self-reported potential violations, including under the EOEP.

name: Regulatory Penalties and Disallowances
description: Events could result in regulatory penalties and disallowances, particularly if the Utility encounters difficulties in restoring power to customers on a timely basis or if related losses are found to be the result of the Utility's practices or failure of electric and other equipment.

name: Regulatory Rate Risk
description: The CPUC and FERC periodically determine revenue requirements and rates of return. Changes in authorized rates or unfavorable outcomes in GRC, cost of capital, or TO rate case proceedings could adversely affect the Utility's revenues and financial results.

name: Regulatory Ratemaking Risk
description: The timing and outcome of ratemaking and other regulatory proceedings can affect the Utility's ability to recover its operating expenses and capital costs. The outcome can be affected by intervening parties' testimonies, potential rate impacts, and the regulatory and political environments.

name: Regulatory Recovery Risk
description: The Utility will be permitted to recover wildfire-related claims and legal fees through rates only if the CPUC or FERC determines the Utility has met the prudency standard, which has not been interpreted or applied by the CPUC, risking partial or full non-recovery of recorded receivables.

name: Regulatory Recovery Uncertainty
description: There is uncertainty around CPUC approval and the timing of final decisions on revenue requirement recovery through Wildfire Mitigation and Catastrophic Events and California Energy Management Assistance applications.

name: Regulatory Risk
description: New or changing laws and regulations, including changes to their interpretation and implementation, as well as increased penalties and enforcement actions for non-compliance, could have a material adverse impact on the company's business, results of operations, and financial condition.

name: Regulatory Risk - CFPB
description: The CFPB has significant authority to regulate consumer financial products in the U.S., including credit, deposits, and payments, and the CFPB and similar agencies may promulgate rules and regulations that affect PayPal's business.

name: Regulatory Scrutiny
description: The global payments industry is increasingly subject to regulatory scrutiny and oversight.

name: Regulatory Scrutiny and Antitrust Risk
description: The company is subject to scrutiny by various government agencies regarding antitrust and competition laws and regulations in the U.S. and internationally, including in connection with proposed or implemented business combinations, acquisitions, investments, partnerships, commercial agreements and business practices.

name: Regulatory Scrutiny and Licensing Risk
description: Frequent or persistent site interruptions could lead to regulatory scrutiny, significant fines and penalties, mandatory changes to business practices, and could cause loss of existing licenses or prevent obtaining additional licenses.

name: Regulatory and Earnings Risk from Operating Expenses
description: If the Utility spends more than CPUC-authorized amounts on operating and maintenance expenses, the excess spending could negatively impact earnings.

name: Regulatory and Legal Investigations
description: PG&E Corporation and the Utility have received and responded to document, data, and other information requests from the CPUC's SED, the DOJ, and law enforcement agencies investigating wildfires. They could be subject to additional investigations, lawsuits, or enforcement actions.

name: Regulatory and Legal Risk
description: Unfavorable regulations, laws, decisions, or interpretations by government or regulatory authorities could cause Amazon to incur substantial costs, expose it to civil and criminal liability or penalties, diminish demand for products and services, increase cost of doing business, or require changes to business practices.

name: Regulatory and Legislative Compliance Risk
description: Operations are subject to extensive federal, state, and local laws, permits, and regulatory requirements, including determinations by the CPUC, CEC, California State Lands Commission, and California Coastal Commission regarding extended nuclear operations.

name: Regulatory and Licensing Risk
description: Obtaining and maintaining lending licenses required to originate loans would be costly, time-consuming and uncertain, and would subject the company to additional laws and regulatory requirements, significantly increasing costs and compliance obligations.

name: Regulatory and Litigation Risk from Wildfire Mitigation
description: PG&E Corporation and the Utility could be subject to additional investigations, regulatory proceedings, enforcement actions, litigation, and customer claims resulting from wildfire mitigation initiatives. These actions could lead to fines, penalties, customer rebates, or failure to obtain cost recovery.

name: Regulatory and Media Scrutiny Risk
description: The technology industry, including the Company, is subject to intense media, political, and regulatory scrutiny, exposing the Company to increasing regulation, government investigations, legal actions, and penalties.

name: Regulatory and Reputational Risk
description: Wildfires have had and could continue to have adverse consequences on the Utility's proceedings with the CPUC and the FERC, and future regulatory proceedings. PG&E Corporation and the Utility may also suffer additional reputational harm and face a more challenging operating, political, and regulatory environment.

name: Release of Hazardous Substances
description: Release of hazardous or toxic substances into the air, water, or soil, including gas leaks, flaking lead-based paint, and leaking insulating fluid from electrical equipment.

name: Reliance on Financing Activities
description: The Company continues to rely on proceeds from financing activities to support its ongoing expenditures, contributing to a working capital deficit.

name: Reliance on Third Parties for Technology
description: The company relies in part on third parties, including some competitors, for the development of and access to new or evolving technologies. These third parties may restrict or prevent access to those technologies.

name: Renewable Energy Target Compliance Risk
description: The Utility may incur additional costs to procure renewable energy to meet new renewable energy targets set by SB 350 and SB 100, and may be subject to penalties for failure to meet the higher targets.

name: Renewable Portfolio Standard Compliance Risk
description: California's RPS program requires utilities to gradually increase renewable energy delivered to customers, with targets of 50% by 2026, 60% by 2030, 90% by 2035, 95% by 2040, and 100% from eligible renewables and zero-carbon resources by 2045.

name: Repatriation costs
description: The company would incur significant additional costs upon repatriation of foreign subsidiary earnings and capital from certain jurisdictions.

name: Reputation and Brand Damage Risk
description: Reputation and brands could be damaged due to scrutiny or backlash from customers, partners, employees, government entities, media, or advocacy groups, stemming from new features, products, services, terms of service changes, or decisions regarding user privacy, data practices, or information security. Social media may amplify negative brand events.

name: Reputation and User Engagement Risk
description: Reputation or user engagement may be negatively affected by hostile or inappropriate activity from third parties, including impersonation, misleading information, or misuse of products and services.

name: Reseller Dependency Risk
description: The financial condition of resellers could weaken, resellers could stop distributing the Company's products, or uncertainty regarding demand could cause resellers to reduce their ordering and marketing of the Company's products.

name: Reseller Investment Risk
description: Programs to enhance reseller sales, including staffing selected resellers' stores and improving product placement displays, can require substantial investment while not assuring return or incremental sales.

name: Responsible AI Use Risk
description: Concerns relating to the responsible use of new and evolving technologies, such as AI, in products and services may result in reputational and financial harm and liability, and may cause the company to incur costs to resolve such issues.

name: Responsible use of AI technologies
description: Issues relating to the responsible use of our technologies, including AI, may result in reputational and financial harm and liability.

name: Restrictions on Technology Development and Adoption
description: Ability to develop, provide, or incorporate new technologies may be limited or restricted by industry-wide standards, platform providers, payments networks, laws and regulations, changing customer expectations, third-party intellectual property rights, and other factors.

name: Retail Business Variability
description: Demand for products and services can fluctuate significantly due to seasonality, promotions, product launches, global economic conditions, natural or human-caused disasters, extreme weather, or geopolitical events, placing increased strain on operations.

name: Retail Competition Risk
description: Retail competition from direct access, community choice aggregation, distributed generation, self-generation, and governmental entities reduces the number of utility customers, leading to a reduction in the Utility's rate base.

name: Retail Operations Risk
description: Retail operations face risks related to managing construction and operating costs, maintaining relationships with existing retail partners, fluctuations in retail inventory value, and obtaining and renewing leases in quality locations at reasonable cost.

name: Retail Store Risks
description: Retail operations are subject to many factors that pose risks and uncertainties, including macroeconomic factors that could adversely affect general retail activity and the Company's ability to manage costs.

name: Retirement Eligibility Risk
description: Approximately 19% of PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's employees are eligible to retire, which could impact workforce stability.

name: Revenue Decline from eBay Marketplace
description: A decline in total payment volume and revenue from core PayPal products and services has occurred, driven primarily by decreased revenue earned on eBay's marketplace platform.

name: Revenue Growth Sustainability
description: Revenue and operating profit growth depends on continued growth in demand for the company's products and services and is affected by general economic, business, and geopolitical conditions worldwide. A softening of demand may result in decreased revenue or slower growth.

name: Revenue Recognition Complexity
description: Revenue recognition for customer agreements is complex due to the identification of distinct performance obligations, timing of recognition, and estimation of variable consideration, requiring extensive audit effort and a high degree of auditor judgment.

name: Revenue Recognition Judgment
description: Significant judgment is required to determine whether products and services are distinct performance obligations, affecting how and when revenue is recognized.

name: Revenue Recognition Risk
description: Complex arrangements with nonstandard terms and conditions may require significant contract interpretation, and determining principal versus agent status and the treatment of incentive payments requires considerable judgment, both of which impact revenue recognized.

name: Revenue Share Risk
description: If the overall return on PayPal branded credit programs does not meet the minimum return threshold in a quarter, revenue share for that period would be zero. If the threshold is not met over four consecutive quarters and the following quarter, PayPal would be required to make a payment to the partner institution.

name: Revenue Shift Risk
description: Office Commercial revenue is affected by the continued shift from Office licensed on-premises to Office 365 subscriptions.

name: Revenue Shortfall and Spending Adjustment
description: Inability to adjust spending to offset revenue shortfalls due to the multi-year development cycle for some products and services.

name: Rising Customer Rates
description: Rising rates for the Utility's customers could result in circumstances in which the Utility is unable to fully recover costs or earn its authorized ROE.

name: Rising Customer Rates and Cost Recovery Risk
description: As rate pressures increase, the likelihood of disallowance or non-recovery may increase. If the CPUC fails to adjust the Utility's rates to reflect the impact of events or conditions caused by climate change, financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows could be materially affected.

name: Rising Energy Rates and Declining Customer Usage
description: If rates rise too rapidly, customer usage could decline, decreasing the volume of sales and further increasing rates in a self-reinforcing feedback loop.

name: Rising Interest Rates and Borrowing Costs
description: Economic conditions and actions by policymaking bodies are contributing to rising interest rates and significant capital market volatility, which could increase the company's future borrowing costs.

name: Rising Rates Risk
description: Rising rates for the Utility's customers could result in circumstances in which the Utility is unable to fully recover costs or earn its authorized ROE.

name: Russia Operations Risk
description: NVIDIA stopped direct sales to Russia and closed its business operations there in FY2023. Revenue to partners that sell into Russia may have been negatively impacted due to the war in Ukraine.

name: Russia-Ukraine Conflict Risk
description: Russia's military hostilities in Ukraine beginning in February 2022 led the company to suspend transactional services in Russia. The total potential financial impact of this situation cannot be reasonably estimated at this time.

name: SB 100 Compliance Risk
description: SB 100 (the 100 Percent Clean Energy Act of 2018) increased the percentage from 50% to 60% of California's electricity portfolio that must come from renewables by 2030 and established state policy that 100% of all retail electricity sales must come from renewable or carbon-free resources by 2045. Failure to comply could result in material fines imposed on PG&E Corporation and the Utility.

name: SB 901 Securitization Risk
description: SB 901 securitization charges, net of related regulatory asset and liability recognized pursuant to the CHT decision, negatively impacted earnings by $608 million in 2022.

name: SEC Securities Classification Risk
description: If the SEC were to assert that any of the cryptocurrencies supported are securities, it could assert that activities involving that cryptocurrency require securities broker-dealer registration or other obligations under federal securities laws.

name: SSP Determination Risk
description: Judgment is required to determine the standalone selling price (SSP) for each distinct performance obligation, particularly when products or services are not sold separately.

name: Safety Culture Investigation
description: The CPUC has opened a formal investigation into whether the organizational culture and governance of PG&E Corporation and the Utility adequately prioritize safety, direct resources appropriately, and achieve accountability and safety goals.

name: Safety Culture OII Risk
description: The Utility could incur material charges, including fines and other penalties, in connection with the Safety Culture OII and other matters that the CPUC's Safety and Enforcement Division may be investigating.

name: Safety Performance Risk
description: The company faces risk related to ensuring safe performance across all work activities, requiring continuous improvement in safety behaviors and ongoing risk reduction measures.

name: Sales Channel Disruption
description: The product distribution system relies on an extensive partner and retail network, and economic conditions impacting partners—such as the bankruptcy of a major distributor, OEM, or retailer—could cause significant sales channel disruption.

name: Search Advertising Growth Dependency
description: Growth depends on ability to attract new users, understand intent, and match intent with relevant content on advertising offerings.

name: Seasonal Demand Risk
description: The Company has historically experienced higher net sales in its first quarter compared to other quarters in its fiscal year due in part to seasonal holiday demand, making results subject to seasonal fluctuations.

name: Seasonal Demand Variability
description: Revenues are generally higher during winter months (November to March) because of higher demand due to heating, creating seasonal variability in financial performance.

name: Seasonal Staffing Risk
description: Inability to adequately staff fulfillment network and customer service centers during peak periods; delivery and other fulfillment companies and customer service co-sourcers may be unable to meet seasonal demand.

name: Seasonality
description: The company's business is affected by seasonality, which historically results in higher sales volume during the fourth quarter ending December 31.

name: Seasonality and Product Introduction Timing
description: The Company has historically experienced higher net sales in its first fiscal quarter due to seasonal holiday demand, and new product introductions can significantly impact net sales, cost of sales and operating expenses.

name: Security Measures Cost
description: The cost of implementing measures to protect products and customer-facing services against security threats could reduce the company's operating margins.

name: Security Vulnerabilities
description: The company may be unable to detect all vulnerabilities in its security controls, systems, or software, including third-party software, which could result in reputational and financial harm.

name: Security Vulnerabilities in Third-Party Components
description: The company's products operate in conjunction with third-party products and components, and a security vulnerability in one of these components could lead to increased costs, liability claims, reduced revenue, or harm to the company's reputation or competitive position.

name: Security Vulnerability Risk
description: Undiscovered vulnerabilities in products or services could result in loss of data or intangible property, or expose end customers to unscrupulous third parties who develop and deploy malicious software programs that could attack products or services.

name: Semiconductor Shortage Risk
description: The global semiconductor industry has experienced high demand and supply shortages, which adversely affected the Company's ability to obtain sufficient quantities of components and products on commercially reasonable terms or at all.

name: Sensitive Data Obligations Risk
description: The Company is subject to specific obligations relating to sensitive information under applicable laws, such as health data, financial data, and biometric data. Failure to comply can result in litigation, government investigations, and significant fees or fines.

name: Service Quality and Outages
description: The Company's service offerings can have quality issues and from time to time experience outages, service slowdowns or errors, resulting in services not performing as anticipated and potentially not meeting customer expectations.

name: Services Competition
description: The Company's services face substantial competition from companies with significant resources, established service offerings with large customer bases, business models providing free content, and illegitimate means to obtain third-party digital content and applications.

name: Severe Weather and Climate Change Risk
description: Extreme weather, drought and shifting climate patterns have intensified challenges including wildfire management in California. More than 81% of California is experiencing severe to extreme drought as of December 31, 2022. Approximately half of the Utility's service area faces elevated or extreme fire danger per CPUC's 2018 statewide fire-threat map.

name: Severe Weather and Natural Disasters
description: Severe weather events and natural disasters—including wildfires, storms, tornadoes, floods, extreme heat, drought, earthquakes, lightning, tsunamis, rising sea levels, pandemics, solar events, electromagnetic events, and wind events—could result in severe business disruptions, prolonged power outages, property damage, injuries, and loss of life. Such events could also cause significant decreases in revenues and earnings as well as substantial additional costs.

name: Share Dilution Risk
description: PG&E Corporation may be required to issue additional shares with respect to HoldCo Rescission or Damage Claims, resulting in dilution to existing common stockholders, or to pay a material amount of cash with respect to allowed Subordinated Debt Claims.

name: Shareholder Control Limitations
description: California law and certain provisions in the Amended Articles and Amended Bylaws of PG&E Corporation may prevent efforts by shareholders to change the direction or management of PG&E Corporation, including provisions that make acquisition more difficult without Board approval.

name: Shareholder Dilution
description: The company seeks to efficiently manage shareholder dilution while maintaining flexibility to issue shares for strategic purposes such as financings, acquisitions, and aligning employee compensation with shareholders' interests.

name: Shareholder restrictions
description: Restrictions on shareholders' ability to change the direction or management of PG&E Corporation may limit investor influence over corporate governance.

name: Shipping Cost Increases
description: Shipping costs are expected to continue increasing as customers utilize shipping offers at a growing rate, more expensive and faster delivery methods are employed, and additional services are offered.

name: Shipping Dependency Risk
description: The company relies on a limited number of shipping companies to deliver inventory, and an inability to negotiate acceptable terms or performance problems with these carriers could negatively impact operating results and customer experience.

name: Short-Term Contract Dependency
description: Substantially all natural gas was purchased under contracts with a term of one year or less, exposing the Utility to market price volatility and supply uncertainty.

name: Singapore Payment Services Act Compliance Risk
description: Upon PayPal Pte. Ltd. obtaining the Major Payment Institution license, new regulatory requirements must be complied with, resulting in increased operational complexity and costs for Singapore and international operations.

name: Single Product Concentration Risk
description: The Company generates a significant portion of its net sales from a single product and a decline in demand for that product could significantly impact quarterly net sales.

name: Single or Limited Source Components
description: Certain components essential to the Company's business are currently obtained from single or limited sources, making them subject to industry-wide shortage and significant commodity pricing fluctuations.

name: Single-Source Supply Risk
description: The Company relies on single-source outsourcing partners in the U.S., Asia, and Europe to supply and manufacture many components, which can negatively impact the cost or supply of components or finished goods if those partners fail to perform.

name: Software Quality and Reliability Problems
description: Sophisticated software may contain bugs and other defects that interfere with intended operation, causing reduced sales, reputational damage, repair costs, delays in new product releases, or legal liability.

name: Spent Nuclear Fuel Storage
description: The Utility constructed interim dry cask storage facilities to store spent fuel onsite at Diablo Canyon and Humboldt Bay due to DOE's failure to fulfill its contractual obligation to take possession of spent nuclear fuel. Considerable uncertainty continues to exist regarding when and whether the DOE will meet its contractual obligation.

name: Standalone Software Revenue Risk
description: New business models or strategies for standalone software solutions and as-a-service offerings may not be successful and NVIDIA may fail to sell any meaningful standalone software or as-a-service solutions, incurring significant costs without achieving significant revenue.

name: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC) Compliance Risk
description: The European Commission imposed new SCC requirements in June 2021 which impose certain contract and operational requirements on PayPal, its merchants, and vendors, potentially requiring new contractual arrangements to avoid limitations on PayPal's ability to process EEA data in countries outside of the EEA.

name: State Privacy Legislation Risk
description: Virginia, Colorado, Utah and Connecticut have passed privacy legislation effective in 2023. Similar laws are being considered in other states at federal and local levels, increasing risk of enforcement action.

name: Statewide Electrical Blackout Risk
description: A prolonged statewide electrical blackout could result in damage to the Utility's equipment or damage to property owned by customers or other third parties.

name: Stock Dilution Risk
description: Issuance of additional shares of PG&E Corporation common stock to satisfy HoldCo Rescission or Damage Claims, or for future financings, acquisitions, or convertible securities, may dilute existing shareholders and reduce book value, market price, and proportionate ownership and voting power.

name: Stock Price Volatility
description: The stock market has experienced extreme price and volume fluctuations that have often been unrelated or disproportionate to the operating performance of companies, which may harm the market price of Class A common stock and Class C capital stock.

name: Strategic Investment Valuation Risk
description: The financial success of investments in privately held companies depends on liquidity events such as public offerings, acquisitions, or private sales. A hypothetical adverse change of 10% in the carrying value of strategic investments as of December 31, 2022 would have resulted in an incremental decrease of approximately $215 million to the carrying value of the portfolio.

name: Strategic Investments Impairment Risk
description: The company holds strategic investments in non-marketable equity securities measured at cost minus impairment, primarily in privately held companies, and is required to regularly review these investments to determine whether impairment has occurred.

name: Strategic Transactions Risk
description: Strategic transactions are inherently risky, may not be successful, and could harm PayPal's business, results of operations, and financial condition. Strategic investments involving minority ownership stakes provide a lesser degree of influence over the relevant business operations.

name: Subordinated Debt Claims Payment Risk
description: PG&E Corporation may be required to pay a material amount of cash with respect to allowed Subordinated Debt Claims, which may have a material adverse impact on PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows.

name: Substantial Indebtedness Risk
description: PG&E Corporation and the Utility carry substantial indebtedness secured by liens on certain assets, which limits their ability to refinance, borrow additional amounts, and use operating cash flow flexibly, while increasing vulnerability to adverse economic conditions and catastrophic events.

name: Succession Planning Risk
description: Failure to ensure effective transfer of knowledge and smooth transitions involving key employees could hinder strategic planning and execution.

name: Supplier Code of Conduct Violations
description: While the Company relies on its partners to adhere to its supplier code of conduct, violations occur from time to time and can materially adversely affect the Company's business, reputation, results of operations, and financial condition.

name: Supplier Commitment Risk
description: The company enters into supplier commitments for certain electronic device components and products based on forecasted customer demand, and if those commitments are reduced, additional costs may be incurred.

name: Supplier Dependency Risk
description: Qualifying a new foundry or subcontractor may result in additional expense and production delays, and NVIDIA has limited direct control over product quantity, quality, and delivery schedules, with risk that suppliers may fail to provide high-quality products.

name: Supplier Relationship Risk
description: The company depends on significant suppliers, including limited or single-source suppliers, without long-term supply agreements in place. Supplier bankruptcies, disasters, or violations of laws or ethical standards could disrupt sourcing and harm the company's reputation.

name: Supply Agreement Renewal Risk
description: There can be no guarantee that the Company will be able to extend or renew supply agreements on similar terms, or at all.

name: Supply Chain Concentration Risk
description: The Company relies on single or limited sources for the supply and manufacture of many critical components. Substantially all manufacturing is performed by outsourcing partners located primarily in China mainland, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan and Vietnam. A business interruption affecting such sources would exacerbate any negative consequences.

name: Supply Chain Constraints
description: Limited supply or capacity in the supply chain has led to extended lead times of more than 12 months, requiring premiums and deposits to secure future supply and capacity, thereby increasing product costs.

name: Supply Chain Cyber Risk
description: Reliance on third-party suppliers for critical business systems, including cloud-based infrastructure, encryption, authentication technology, and employee email, exposes the company to supply-chain attacks or business disruptions if those third parties have inadequate security measures or compromised systems.

name: Supply Chain Cyberattacks
description: The company is subject to supply chain cyberattacks in which malware may be introduced to customers through compromised software updates from a software provider.

name: Supply Chain Disruption
description: Risk of inability to find replacement vendors on favorable terms or at all in the event of a supply chain disruption, including volume-based pricing issues and minimum purchase requirements.

name: Supply Chain Risk
description: Supply has grown significantly due to current supply chain conditions, product complexity, and recent reductions in demand, with inventory provisions for excess inventory and purchase obligations totaling $2.17 billion in FY2023.

name: Supply Constraint Risk
description: Risk that foundry partners and contract manufacturers may not have adequate capacity or may experience supply constraints, including due to natural disasters, pandemics, or geopolitical tensions.

name: Supply Exceeding Demand
description: In fiscal year 2023, NVIDIA's supply exceeded demand in several product areas, causing revenue to deviate from historical seasonal patterns.

name: Supply Order Commitment Risk
description: The company faces risk from non-cancellable supply orders or prepayment requirements that may result in excess inventory or financial losses if demand or requirements change unexpectedly.

name: Supply Shortages and Price Increases
description: The Company remains subject to significant risks of supply shortages and price increases that can materially adversely affect its business, results of operations, and financial condition, particularly as new products often utilize custom components available from only one source.

name: System Failures and Cyberattacks
description: Company has experienced and expects to continue to experience system failures, cyberattacks, unplanned outages that interrupt availability or reduce speed/functionality of products and services, resulting in loss of revenue.

name: System Interruption and Lack of Redundancy
description: The company experiences occasional system interruptions and delays that make websites and services unavailable or slow to respond, preventing efficient order fulfillment or service provision. Systems are not fully redundant and disaster recovery planning may not be sufficient to address all potential disruptions.

name: System Interruptions Risk
description: If too many customers access websites within a short period due to increased demand, system interruptions may make websites unavailable or prevent efficient order fulfillment, reducing the volume of goods offered or sold.

name: System Upgrade and Re-platforming Risk
description: System upgrades and re-platforming efforts are costly and time-consuming, involve significant technical complexity and risk, may divert resources from new features and products, and may ultimately not be effective.

name: Systems Failures and Insurance Insufficiency
description: Insurance coverage may be insufficient to compensate for losses caused by interruptions in service due to systems failures and similar events.

name: Talent Acquisition and Retention Risk
description: Difficulty in recruiting and retaining highly skilled workers and key leaders may adversely affect the ability to develop and deliver successful products and services.

name: Talent Attraction and Retention
description: Competition for key and highly skilled personnel is intense, especially for executive talent, software engineers, and other technology talent. The company may be limited in its ability to recruit or hire internationally due to restrictive immigration laws or policies.

name: Talent Competition
description: As a leading technology platform, PayPal competes for top global talent, making talent acquisition, development, and retention a key challenge.

name: Talent Recruitment and Retention Risk
description: Risk of failing to recruit, develop, and retain talented employees including executives, scientists, engineers, and technical staff in a competitive global talent market.

name: Talent Retention Risk
description: Performance depends on talents of highly skilled individuals. Competition for qualified employees is intense, and compensation arrangements may not always be successful in attracting and retaining employees.

name: Talent Retention and Compensation Costs
description: In fiscal year 2023, base salaries of salaried employees were frozen at fiscal year 2022 levels to manage costs in a competitive environment, potentially impacting talent retention.

name: Tax Act Impact Risk
description: Risk related to the impact of the Tax Act on TO18 rates in January and February 2018, subject to ongoing appeals.

name: Tax Audit Risk
description: The company is undergoing investigations, audits, and reviews by tax authorities in multiple U.S. and foreign tax jurisdictions. Any adverse outcome could result in unforeseen tax-related liabilities that differ from the amounts recorded in financial statements.

name: Tax Compliance Risk
description: The company faces risks related to new obligations to withhold or collect sales, consumption, value-added, or other taxes on online marketplaces and remote sellers, including taxes on online advertising and marketplace service revenues in non-U.S. jurisdictions.

name: Tax Controversies
description: The company is subject to tax controversies in various jurisdictions that may result in additional income tax liabilities. Developments in audits, investigations, or other tax controversies could have a material effect on operating results or cash flows.

name: Tax Controversy Risk
description: The company is currently subject to tax controversies in various jurisdictions, which may have a material impact on its financial condition or results of operations.

name: Tax Examination Risk
description: Subject to continuous examination of income tax returns by the IRS and other domestic and foreign tax authorities, with risk of adverse outcomes affecting provision for income taxes.

name: Tax Expense and Liability Risk
description: The company's tax expense and liabilities are affected by changes in business operations, acquisitions, investments, intercompany transactions, foreign earnings, currency exchange rates, and changes in tax assets and liabilities.

name: Tax Law Changes
description: Changes in tax laws, exposure to unanticipated additional tax liabilities, or implementation of reporting or record-keeping obligations could have a material adverse effect on the business. Various countries have proposed or enacted digital services taxes.

name: Tax Legislation Risk
description: The 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act requires capitalization and amortization of R&D expenditures beginning in FY2023, which impacts NVIDIA's effective tax rate.

name: Tax Liabilities
description: Operating results may be adversely impacted by additional tax liabilities, higher than expected tax rates and other tax-related factors.

name: Tax Liability Risk
description: Tax benefits of $1.02 billion, including related interest and penalties of $95 million, were recorded in non-current income tax payable at the end of FY2023, and NVIDIA is currently under IRS examination for fiscal years 2018 and 2019.

name: Tax Rate Changes and Additional Tax Liabilities
description: The Company is subject to changes in tax rates, adoption of new U.S. or international tax legislation, and exposure to additional tax liabilities across multiple jurisdictions including Ireland and Singapore. Effective tax rates are affected by changes in earnings mix, deferred tax asset valuations, new taxes, and tax law changes.

name: Tax Rate Risk
description: Future effective tax rates could be adversely affected by lower-than-anticipated earnings in low-tax jurisdictions, higher-than-anticipated earnings in high-tax jurisdictions, net gains and losses on hedges, changes in deferred tax asset or liability valuations, or changes in tax laws, regulations, or accounting principles.

name: Tax Rate Variability
description: The company's effective tax rate is subject to variability due to factors including intangible property transfers, stock-based compensation tax benefits, the mix of income between U.S. and foreign jurisdictions, and regulatory changes such as TCJA provisions.

name: Tax and Stock-Based Compensation Risk
description: Risk of reduced future tax benefits from stock-based compensation, increased effective tax rate, and adverse effect on financial results.

name: Tax-related risks
description: Tax-related risks and uncertainties include the grantor trust election for the Fire Victim Trust and the potential inability to use net operating loss carryforwards and other tax attributes to offset future income.

name: Technological Change Risk
description: Competition expected to increase from both existing competitors and new market entrants with products that may be lower priced or provide better performance or additional features.

name: Technological and Decarbonization Changes
description: The electric power and gas industries are undergoing significant changes driven by technological advancements and a decarbonized economy, including increasing deployment of distributed energy resources such as on-site solar generation, electric vehicles, energy storage, and fuel cells.

name: Technology Development Complexity Risk
description: Developing new technologies is complex and can require long development and testing periods. Significant delays in new releases or significant problems in creating new products or services could adversely affect revenue.

name: Telecommunications Regulation Risk
description: Laws in several jurisdictions, including EU Member State laws under the European Electronic Communications Code, increasingly classify certain services as regulated telecommunications services, resulting in additional data protection, security, and law enforcement surveillance obligations.

name: Temporary Permissions Regime Authorization Risk
description: PayPal (Europe) currently operates in the U.K. under the Temporary Permissions Regime, and if it is unable to obtain required authorizations before the expiry of longstop dates set by U.K. regulators, its European operations could lose the ability to offer services within or into the U.K. market.

name: Third-Party Confidential Information Exposure
description: The Company's business requires it to share confidential information with suppliers and other third parties. The Company relies on global suppliers that are also exposed to ransomware and other malicious attacks that can disrupt business operations.

name: Third-Party Construction Damage
description: Construction performed by third parties that damages the Utility's underground or overhead facilities, including dig-ins that damage underground pipelines.

name: Third-Party Content Licensing Risk
description: Licensing or distribution arrangements for third-party content can be for short time periods and do not guarantee continuation or renewal on commercially reasonable terms. Third-party content providers may offer competing products or limit the Company's access to content.

name: Third-Party Contractor Liability
description: The Utility may retain liability for contractor work quality and completion, and can be subject to penalties or enforcement actions due to contractor violations or personal injury caused by contractor actions.

name: Third-Party Custodian Risk
description: Customer cryptocurrency offerings expose the company to financial and third-party risks including inappropriate access to, theft, or destruction of cryptocurrency assets held by a custodian, insufficient insurance coverage, failure to maintain effective controls, inability to purchase or liquidate cryptocurrency holdings, and defaults on financial or performance obligations.

name: Third-Party Dependency Risk
description: Reliance on third parties in many aspects of business, including networks, banks, payment processors, payment gateways, unaffiliated third-party lenders, cryptocurrency custodial service providers, and external business partners, which creates additional risk.

name: Third-Party Developer Dependency
description: NVIDIA depends on developers and other third parties to build accelerated computing applications that leverage its platforms. Failure by developers or content providers to do so could adversely affect customer demand.

name: Third-Party IP Infringement Claims
description: Third parties may allege that the company is infringing their patents and other intellectual property rights.

name: Third-Party Intellectual Property Access Risk
description: The Company relies on access to third-party intellectual property, which may not be available on commercially reasonable terms or at all. Failure to obtain such rights can preclude the Company from selling certain products or services.

name: Third-Party Inventory Management Risk
description: Under commercial agreements, the company maintains inventory on behalf of other companies, increasing the complexity of inventory tracking. Failure to properly handle such inventory or inaccurate demand forecasting may result in insufficient storage space or an inability to optimize the fulfillment network.

name: Third-Party Litigation Risk
description: The Company has commercial relationships with other companies in the technology industry that are or may become subject to investigations and litigation. If resolved against those other companies, such proceedings could materially adversely affect the Company.

name: Third-Party Provider Dependency
description: Engaging and relying upon third-party providers exposes PayPal to legal, regulatory, information security, reputational, and operational risks, including reliance on a small number of third-party payment processors and risks associated with transitioning payment processing arrangements.

name: Third-Party Seller Program Cost Risk
description: As third-party seller sales grow, the cost of the program will increase and could negatively affect operating results.

name: Third-Party Supplier Dependency
description: Dependency on third-party suppliers and their technology to manufacture, assemble, test, package or design products reduces control over product quantity and quality, manufacturing yields, development, and product delivery schedules.

name: Third-Party and Vendor Cybersecurity Risk
description: Cyberattacks or data security breaches affecting IT infrastructure of acquired companies, customers, partners, or vendors including data center and cloud computing providers could have negative effects on PayPal.

name: Trade Policy Risk
description: Changes to trade policy or agreements driven by populism, protectionism, or economic nationalism may result in higher tariffs, local sourcing requirements, export controls, investment restrictions, or other developments that make it more difficult to sell products in foreign countries.

name: Trade Restrictions Risk
description: Inability to sell to a customer due to U.S. or other countries' trade restrictions negatively impacting revenue.

name: Trade Sanctions and Export Controls Risk
description: Economic sanctions in the U.S., EU, and other countries prohibit most business with restricted entities or countries, and U.S. export controls restrict Microsoft from offering products and services to certain entities. Non-compliance could result in reputational harm, monetary fines, loss of revenues, or criminal sanctions.

name: Transaction and Credit Losses
description: Transaction and credit losses encompass expenses associated with customer protection programs, fraud, chargebacks, and credit losses on loans receivable. These losses fluctuate based on total payment volume, product mix, macroeconomic conditions, merchant insolvency events, changes to customer protection programs, regulatory changes, and the credit quality of loans receivable.

name: Transfer Pricing Risk
description: The primary unresolved issues in IRS audits relate to transfer pricing, which could have a material impact on consolidated financial statements upon resolution. Microsoft remains under audit for tax years 2004 through 2017.

name: Transportation Cost Recovery Risk
description: The Utility incurs transportation costs under various agreements with interstate and Canadian third-party providers, subject to FERC and National Energy Board tariff approvals.

name: U.S. Cell Phone Litigation
description: Microsoft Mobile Oy, a subsidiary of Microsoft, along with other handset manufacturers and network operators, is a defendant in 46 lawsuits alleging that radio emissions from cellular handsets caused brain tumors and other adverse health effects. Microsoft assumed responsibility for these claims through its acquisition of Nokia's Devices and Services business.

name: Unauthorized Access and Data Breach Risk
description: The Utility and its third-party vendors have been subject to breaches and attempts to gain unauthorized access to IT systems or confidential data. Such events could subject the Utility to significant expenses, claims, government inquiries, penalties for violation of privacy laws, regulatory actions resulting in material fines, loss of customers and reputational harm.

name: Unauthorized Access to Confidential Information
description: Losses or unauthorized access to or releases of confidential information, including personal information, could subject the Company to significant reputational, financial, legal and operational consequences. The Company's business requires it to use and store confidential information with respect to customers and employees.

name: Unauthorized Access to Customer Data
description: Unauthorized parties may gain access to customers' confidential personal or proprietary information and financial instrument data stored on or accessible through information technology systems, which may be used to execute fraudulent transactions or otherwise engage in fraudulent actions.

name: Uncertain Tax Positions
description: Significant judgment is required in evaluating uncertain tax positions and determining provision for income taxes. No assurance can be given that the final tax outcome will not be different from reserved amounts, potentially impacting the provision for income taxes and effective tax rate.

name: Uncertain Tax Positions - Transfer Pricing
description: Long-term income tax liabilities include uncertain tax positions related to unresolved transfer pricing issues with the IRS, with audits covering tax years subsequent to 2003. Resolution of these matters could have a material impact on the company's financial statements.

name: Underground Gas Storage Regulation Risk
description: California Geologic Energy Management Division establishes and enforces regulations for the operation of the Utility's underground gas storage facilities.

name: Unplanned Outage Risk
description: Fixed and volumetric payments may be adjusted downward in the event of extended unplanned outages at Diablo Canyon, and the Utility may also be liable for replacement power costs if it fails to meet the CPUC's reasonable-manager standard.

name: Unsafe Operating Conditions
description: Failure to take action to mitigate unsafe operating conditions, facilities, or equipment, leading to catastrophic events such as wildfires or natural gas explosions.

name: Utility Income Deterioration
description: The deterioration of income from, or other available assets of, the Utility for any reason could limit or impair the Utility's ability to pay dividends or other distributions to PG&E Corporation, which could materially and adversely affect PG&E Corporation's ability to meet its obligations.

name: Valuation Impairment Risk
description: Valuations supporting acquisitions and strategic investments could change rapidly, potentially resulting in impairments or other-than-temporary declines in fair value that could adversely impact financial results.

name: Variable Consideration Estimation
description: Products are sold with rights of return and credits or incentives, requiring estimation of variable consideration, which introduces uncertainty in the amount of revenue recognized.

name: Variable Interest Rate Risk
description: A substantial portion of PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's indebtedness bears interest at variable rates, exposing them to interest rate risk as well as risk from catastrophic events.

name: Vegetation Management Compliance Risk
description: The CPUC placed the Utility into step 1 of the EOEP on April 15, 2021 due to insufficient progress toward approved safety or risk-driven investments related to its electric business, specifically finding that less than five percent of the Enhanced Vegetation Management (EVM) work completed in 2020 was on the 20 highest risk power lines.

name: Vendor Non-Trade Receivables and Supply Agreement Prepayments Concentration Risk
description: The Company has unsecured vendor non-trade receivables and prepayments associated with long-term supply agreements concentrated among a few individual vendors located primarily in Asia, creating concentration risk.

name: Vendor Payment Requirements
description: Vendors have requested and may continue to ask for shorter payment terms, which may impact cash flow generation.

name: WMP Compliance Risk
