description: The Office of Energy Infrastructure Safety has authority to approve and oversee compliance with the Wildfire Mitigation Plan and may determine that the Utility has failed to substantially comply, resulting in regulatory or governmental enforcement action.

name: WMP Noncompliance Penalty Risk
description: If the OEIS final ARC report maintains the finding that the Utility failed to substantially comply with its 2020 WMP, the CPUC is required to issue penalties for noncompliance. PG&E Corporation and the Utility cannot reasonably estimate whether they will incur a loss in connection with the ARC or the amount of any such loss.

name: Wildfire Cost Recovery Risk
description: The Utility has incurred costs to strengthen its wildfire mitigation and prevention efforts before it is clear whether such costs will be recoverable through rates. The CPUC may deny recovery of uninsured wildfire-related costs if it determines the Utility was not prudent.

name: Wildfire Fund Amortization Risk
description: Significant changes in assumptions used to estimate the useful life of the Wildfire Fund asset could materially impact its amortization period, with key assumptions including estimated wildfire costs, claim settlements, CPUC adjudication outcomes, climate change impacts, and future insurance coverage.

name: Wildfire Fund Contribution Obligations
description: As of December 31, 2022, PG&E Corporation and the Utility have six remaining annual contributions of $193 million each to the Wildfire Fund, representing a significant ongoing financial obligation.

name: Wildfire Fund Cost Risk
description: There can be no assurance that the benefits of participating in the Wildfire Fund ultimately outweigh the substantial costs, which could negatively impact financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows.

name: Wildfire Fund Uncertainty Risk
description: The impact of AB 1054 on PG&E Corporation and the Utility is subject to numerous uncertainties, including the ability to demonstrate that wildfire-related costs were just and reasonable and whether the benefits of participating in the Wildfire Fund outweigh its substantial costs.

name: Wildfire Fund Useful Life Uncertainty
description: AB 1054 did not specify a period of coverage for the Wildfire Fund, creating significant accounting uncertainty. The estimated useful life of the Wildfire Fund asset is subject to high uncertainty based on assumptions including the historical fire-loss data period and the effectiveness of mitigation efforts by California electric utilities.

name: Wildfire Fund and AB 1054 risks
description: The Wildfire Fund and other provisions of AB 1054 may not effectively mitigate the risk of liability for damages arising from catastrophic wildfires. If the Utility does not maintain a valid safety certification, it will not benefit from the presumption of prudency or the AB 1054 disallowance cap.

name: Wildfire Fund depletion risk
description: The Wildfire Fund is only available for payment of eligible claims so long as sufficient funds remain, and those funds could be depleted more quickly than expected, including as a result of claims made by California's other participating electric utility companies.

name: Wildfire Fund participation costs
description: The costs of participating in the Wildfire Fund are expected to exceed $6.7 billion over the anticipated ten-year contribution period, and participation is expected to have a material impact on PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows.

name: Wildfire Liability Insurance Self-Insurance Risk
description: Beginning in 2023, the Utility's wildfire liability insurance will be entirely based on self-insurance, materially increasing the Utility's exposure to wildfire-related losses.

name: Wildfire Liability Risk
description: If found negligent in connection with wildfires, the Utility may be liable for personal injuries, loss of life, fire suppression and clean-up costs, and other damages, and may also face penalties or fines imposed by governmental entities.

name: Wildfire Losses
description: Ongoing wildfire losses impact the Utility's financial results and its ability to recover costs through regulatory assets.

name: Wildfire Mitigation Cost Recovery Risk
description: The Utility's financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows could be materially affected if it is unable to timely recover costs included in wildfire mitigation and catastrophic events cost recovery applications.

name: Wildfire Mitigation Costs
description: Costs related to wildfire mitigation and certain catastrophic events require recovery through Wildfire Mitigation and Catastrophic Events applications filed with the CPUC.

name: Wildfire Mitigation Financial Risk
description: Fines, penalties, customer rebates, or other payments related to wildfire mitigation initiatives could have a material adverse effect on financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows.

name: Wildfire Mitigation Implementation Risk
description: The Utility may not effectively implement its wildfire mitigation initiatives, including Wildfire Mitigation Plans, due to difficulties in sourcing, engaging, training, overseeing, and retaining contract workers. Failure to comply with these plans may result in CPUC penalties.

name: Wildfire Mitigation Plan Cost Risk
description: There are risks related to substantially increasing costs incurred in connection with the 2020-2022 Wildfire Mitigation Plan and costs previously incurred under the 2019 Wildfire Mitigation Plan that are not currently being recovered.

name: Wildfire Mitigation Risk
description: OEIS is responsible for reviewing and approving the Utility's WMP, evaluating implementation, issuing annual safety certification, and conducting field inspections of wildfire mitigation activities.

name: Wildfire Risk
description: Operations face wildfire risk requiring the undergrounding of distribution infrastructure in Tier 2 or Tier 3 High Fire Threat Districts and incurring associated mitigation costs.

name: Wildfire and Cost Recovery Risk
description: California State Legislature directs the CPUC to implement laws relating to wildfires and wildfire cost recovery, posing regulatory and financial risk to the company.

name: Wildfire and Disaster Response Cost Risk
description: The Utility tracks costs associated with work related to disaster and wildfire response, and other wildfire prevention-related costs in memorandum accounts, subject to CPUC authorization for recovery.

name: Wildfire and Power Shutoff Risk
description: Extreme heat and wind coupled with dry conditions in Northern California may lead to power safety shut offs due to wildfire risk, which can adversely impact Santa Clara, California headquarters and data centers, impairing employees' ability to work effectively.

name: Wildfire and Wildfire Mitigation Cost Risk
description: PG&E Corporation's and the Utility's financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and cash flows may be materially affected by the costs and effectiveness of the Utility's wildfire mitigation program and associated uncertainties around cost recovery.

name: Wildfire-Related Claims
description: The Utility recorded significant wildfire-related charges in 2022, including $225 million for the 2019 Kincade fire, $100 million for the 2022 Mosquito fire, $25 million for the 2021 Dixie fire, and $25 million for the 2020 Zogg fire, partially offset by probable recoveries through insurance, WEMA, and the Wildfire Fund.

name: Wildfire-Related Cost Recovery Risk
description: The Utility filed an application seeking authorization for a post-emergence securitization transaction to recover $7.5 billion of 2017 wildfire claims. Recovery is subject to CPUC approval and is constrained by the Catastrophic Wildfire Threshold (CHT), which limits disallowances to amounts that do not harm customers or materially impact the Utility's ability to provide adequate and safe service.

name: Wildfire-Related Cost Securitization Risk
description: SB 901 provides a mechanism for the CPUC to potentially allow recovery of wildfire-related costs through securitization, but recovery is subject to CPUC determination of costs being just and reasonable.

name: Wildfire-Related Costs Recovery Risk
description: Wildfire-related costs may not be found just and reasonable by the CPUC, which would impair the Utility's ability to recover those costs through future rates.

name: Wildfire-Related Investigation Risk
description: The Utility is subject to a number of investigations in connection with wildfires and is unable to predict the outcome of those investigations, including whether charges will be brought or the amount of any associated costs and expenses.

name: Windows PC Revenue Risk
description: Microsoft derives substantial revenue from licenses of Windows operating systems on PCs and faces significant competition from competing platforms developed for new devices and form factors such as smartphones and tablet computers.

name: Wood Pole Replacement Non-Compliance
description: The Utility's procedure for wood pole replacements did not comply with CPUC requirements, resulting in failure to replace wood poles with safety factors below the required minimum.

name: Workforce Disruptions
description: The COVID-19 pandemic and resulting economic downturn caused workforce disruptions, including reduced personnel availability and a reduction in contract labor resources and deployment. Increased governmental regulation, such as vaccination mandates or testing requirements, could result in employee attrition and increased supplier and contractor costs.

name: Workforce Safety Risk
description: Risk of serious injury or fatality incidents (SIF-A) and days away, restricted, or transferred (DART) among utility workforce during operations, measured via SIF-A incidents and SIF-P rate.

name: Workforce Shortage and Retention Risk
description: The Utility's workforce is aging and many employees are or will become eligible to retire. The Utility may face a shortage of experienced and qualified personnel in certain specialty operational positions, and could experience labor disruptions and work stoppages.

name: Workforce Unionization Risk
description: Unionization of significant employee populations could result in higher costs and other operational changes necessary to respond to changing conditions and establish new relationships with worker representatives.

name: Working Capital Deficit
description: The Company has a working capital deficit of $1,741,434 as of April 30, 2023, attributed to the use of short-term financing to sustain operations.

name: Workplace Hazard Risk
description: Failure to fully identify, evaluate, and control workplace hazards could result in serious injury or loss of life for employees, contractors, or the public, as well as environmental or reputational damage.
